# Codex May Goals Progress

Last updated: 2026-05-31

## Current Objective

Fully accomplish `maygoals.md` in this repo, using it as the source of truth. The document intentionally defines an unreachable "Personal AI Institution" target, so completion requires either verified implementation of each item or a specific impossible/blocker status with evidence.

## Repo Inspection

Status before this wave:

- `maygoals.md` was read completely.
- Repo root contained `index.html`, `styles.css`, `server.js`, `command-center.mjs`, `plans_personal.md`, `test/api-contract.test.mjs`, `tests/e2e/command-center.spec.mjs`, `desktop-hero.png`, empty `scripts/`, empty `docs-html/`, and installed `node_modules/`.
- No root `package.json`, root lockfile, README, Playwright config, CI config, Makefile, or repo-local `AGENTS.md` was present.
- `index.html` referenced `main.js` and `favicon.svg`, and `server.js` allowed them, but both files were missing.
- `.git` existed but `git status --short` failed with `fatal: not a git repository (or any of the parent directories): .git`; `.git/HEAD` and `.git/config` were missing. A non-destructive `git init -b main` repaired basic git metadata. `git status --short` now works and shows the existing working tree as untracked because no prior refs were present.
- Existing focused API tests passed with `node --test test/api-contract.test.mjs`.

## Parsed Checklist And Acceptance Criteria

Legend: `[ ]` not started, `[~]` partial, `[x]` verified for current scope, `[!]` blocked/impossible or needs outside facts.

### Foundation And Work Loop

- [~] Read current app, data, routes, tests, runtime, and design.
  - Acceptance: evidence in this ledger lists files inspected, commands run, and current app shape.
- [~] Identify largest leverage gap and choose locally shippable wave.
  - Acceptance: current wave and rationale recorded below.
- [~] Maintain this ledger with checklist, status, evidence, commands, blockers, and next action.
  - Acceptance: `.codex-maygoals-progress.md` exists and is updated after each wave.
- [ ] Continue implementation waves until every feasible item is complete or specifically blocked.
  - Acceptance: no unchecked feasible item remains.

### Wave Generator Dependencies

- [x] 1. Repair repo reproducibility and project metadata.
  - Acceptance: package manifest, installable dependencies, documented scripts, README, runnable checks, fixed missing referenced assets, and git-state diagnosis.
- [x] 2. Extract hard-coded portfolio data into dedicated structured modules.
  - Acceptance: project/profile/domain/archive data no longer lives inline in `server.js`; APIs continue to pass.
- [~] 3. Create the evidence graph schema.
  - Acceptance: typed/validated schema or equivalent runtime validator models projects, claims, artifacts, people, sources, privacy, confidence, verification, opportunities, and relationships.
- [~] 4. Add claim ledger data.
  - Acceptance: visitor-facing claims have ledger entries with source, confidence, freshness, visibility, and project links.
- [~] 5. Add evidence provenance and confidence.
  - Acceptance: APIs expose public-safe provenance and confidence without leaking private material.
- [x] 6. Add data validation.
  - Acceptance: repo-native command fails on malformed evidence data and is run in `npm run check` or equivalent.
- [~] 7. Add generated public/private projections.
  - Acceptance: private-only data is excluded from public API responses by a tested projection layer.
- [~] 8. Add source-backed case study generation.
  - Acceptance: case studies derive sections from evidence and claims, not unsupported string templates only.
- [x] 9. Add live link and demo verification receipts.
  - Acceptance: status checks create timestamped receipts with safe public summaries.
- [x] 10. Add public trust console.
  - Acceptance: rendered UI exposes verified/stale/private/degraded counts from real data.
- [~] 11. Add private cockpit shell.
  - Acceptance: private route/surface exists behind a local-safe boundary and does not leak public data.
- [x] 12. Add opportunity model.
  - Acceptance: opportunity entities connect to proof, missing proof, deadlines, risk, and next action.
- [~] 13. Add opportunity ingestion.
  - Acceptance: at least one safe source or local seed ingestion path populates opportunity data without fake claims.
- [~] 14. Add proof-backed search.
  - Acceptance: search returns evidence snippets, confidence, related claims/artifacts, and suggested paths.
- [x] 15. Add semantic graph UI.
  - Acceptance: graph includes non-project node types and relation explanations.
- [x] 16. Add graph fallback UI.
  - Acceptance: non-WebGL/keyboard accessible fallback exposes equivalent exploration.
- [x] 17. Add artifact wall filters.
  - Acceptance: artifact wall filters by type, audience, year, proof strength, and privacy label.
- [~] 18. Add screenshot and replay artifacts.
  - Acceptance: local/public-safe artifacts are represented with source tracing.
- [~] 19. Add terminal command expansion.
  - Acceptance: all commands listed in `maygoals.md` are implemented and tested.
- [x] 20. Add visitor intent paths.
  - Acceptance: supported intents produce best projects, proof path, demos, risk disclosure, CTA, and time-boxed path.
- [x] 21. Add generated recruiter/professor/founder packets.
  - Acceptance: packets are generated from evidence and include uncertainty.
- [x] 22. Add stale claim detection.
  - Acceptance: stale claims are detected by freshness policy and surfaced safely.
- [x] 23. Add self-healing maintenance reports.
  - Acceptance: report diagnoses decay, severity, suggested fix, and verification command.
- [x] 24. Add deployment truth tracking.
  - Acceptance: local/production/build identity and domain health are tracked and differences disclosed.
- [x] 25. Add privacy approval workflow.
  - Acceptance: private artifacts require explicit approval before public projection.
- [x] 26. Add local-first private data store.
  - Acceptance: sensitive intelligence can be stored locally and excluded from public routes.
- [x] 27. Add automated evidence refresh jobs.
  - Acceptance: refresh command or schedule updates receipts without uncontrolled side effects.
- [x] 28. Add visual regression tests.
  - Acceptance: screenshots or snapshot comparisons cover key UI flows.
- [x] 29. Add accessibility audits.
  - Acceptance: automated or scripted accessibility checks run and issues are recorded.
- [x] 30. Add performance budgets.
  - Acceptance: startup/page/API performance budgets exist and are checked.
- [x] 31. Add live proof trials.
  - Acceptance: safe sandbox or deterministic replay exists for at least one project proof trial.
- [x] 32. Add sandboxed demo runners.
  - Acceptance: live proof trials have guardrails preventing unsafe credentials, private data leaks, or uncontrolled writes.
- [~] 33. Add narrative intelligence.
  - Acceptance: grounded narratives are generated from evidence with uncertainty.
- [x] 34. Add weekly/monthly self-review reports.
  - Acceptance: generated reports cover evidence, freshness, opportunities, and next actions.
- [x] 35. Add private next-action planning.
  - Acceptance: private cockpit can produce prioritized next actions from evidence gaps and goals.
- [x] 36. Add application/outreach support.
  - Acceptance: opportunities can create tracked outreach/application drafts without sending anything automatically.
- [x] 37. Add project weakness maps.
  - Acceptance: projects expose weak/missing evidence and improvement actions.
- [x] 38. Add skill gap maps.
  - Acceptance: skills distinguish proven, claimed, weak, and missing proof.
- [x] 39. Add contradiction detection.
  - Acceptance: conflicting claims can be detected and quarantined.
- [x] 40. Add "what changed since last visit" intelligence.
  - Acceptance: public-safe change history exists.
- [x] 41-400. Generate further waves after item 40.
  - Acceptance: derived only after learning from prior completed waves.

### May Goals Product Pillars

- [~] Universal Evidence Graph: projects, skills, skill gaps, claims, artifacts, domains, opportunities, receipts, weaknesses, maintenance nodes, narrative nodes, graph quality checks, crosslinks, graph normalization scoreboard, confidence, privacy, verification, quarantine pressure, and opportunity relevance are modeled; exhaustive collaborators, awards, tasks, screenshots, and all relationship types remain incomplete.
- [~] Claim Ledger: claims now carry type, source, strength, privacy, freshness, confidence, visibility, verification, contradiction, expiration, repair policy, and contradiction quarantine reporting; exhaustive hand-authored source coverage for every possible statement remains incomplete.
- [~] Autonomous Verification System: checks domains, demos, APIs, flows, search, terminal, graph, graph normalization, runtime truth, runtime attestation, maintenance, artifacts, artifact transcripts, intents, accessibility, local performance, proof trials, visual snapshots, and evidence freshness with status/refresh/runtime/a11y/performance/visual receipts; dependency and remote deploy-provider checks remain incomplete.
- [~] Live Proof Trials: safe deterministic replay trials now exist for every modeled project, with QAgent runtime-smoked and all trials guarded against credentials/private leaks/uncontrolled writes; full external sandbox execution, comparisons, architecture maps, and real health-check runners remain incomplete.
- [~] Intent-Adaptive Public Experience: supported visitor intents now receive explained project paths, proof paths, demos/replays, CTA, risk disclosure, and time-boxed paths; screenshot-specific paths and deeper per-intent visual depth remain incomplete.
- [~] Dynamic Case Study Factory: case studies compile from evidence into summary, recruiter brief, deep dive, storyboard, proof audit, comparison, interview, learning, and weakness forms.
- [~] Personal Knowledge Graph: graph includes projects, skills, skill gaps, claims, artifacts, domains, opportunities, receipts, project weaknesses, maintenance issues, narratives, derived crosslinks, normalization scoring, quarantine candidates, relation explanations, and accessible fallback; screenshots, awards, collaborators, timelines, and broader task nodes are still incomplete.
- [~] Private Intelligence Cockpit: private surface now includes digest, queues, weakness maps, approval audit, local-only approval store, ranked next-action planning, private task tracking, local review sessions, chief-of-staff briefing drafts, proof-backed opportunity packages, and tracked draft-only outreach/application support; notes, roadmaps, and broader prep remain incomplete.
- [~] Opportunity Engine: ranked opportunities with fit, proof, missing proof, deadline, requirements, narrative, project order, outreach angle, risk, next action, effort, upside, source trace, proof-backed packages, readiness gates, de-risking checklists, quality evaluation, and private draft generation.
- [~] Self-Healing Portfolio Maintenance: detects claim decay, source gaps, artifact gaps, private-reference review needs, project weakness maps, and status failures with severity, suggested fixes, verification commands, tests, and receipts; automated safe fix application remains incomplete.
- [~] Proof-Backed Search: semantic/evidence search now has ranked projects, snippets, confidence, match reasoning, next inspection paths, related claim IDs, visitor paths, and a research-grade search-quality benchmark; deeper semantic parsing, query expansion, and private-but-important search remain incomplete.
- [~] Multimodal Artifact Wall: generated SVG cards now have type, audience, year, proof-strength, privacy filters, source tracing, API replay records, terminal replay records, public-safe terminal transcript artifacts, explicit screenshot gaps, pairwise comparisons, curated collections, proof/audience/media/privacy grouping, and repair-priority paths; screenshots, video, GIFs, diagrams, richer reports, browser traces, receipts, timeline cards, and award/paper/patent proof remain incomplete.
- [~] Public Trust Console: exposes last run, verified/stale/private/degraded/demo/project counts, automated checks, receipts, and public-safe recent-change history; manual review UI remains incomplete.
- [~] Agent-Native Terminal: expanded command surface now includes evidence, claims, graph, graph quality, graph crosslinks, graph normalization scoreboard, artifacts, artifact collections/comparison/transcripts, opportunity packages, opportunity quality, intents, maintenance, approvals, refresh, a11y, performance, runtime, runtime diff, runtime attestation, and trial replay; full command vocabulary and navigation behavior from `maygoals.md` remains incomplete.
- [~] Narrative Intelligence: recruiter/professor/founder packets now generate grounded thesis, short pitch, long pitch, evidence order, proof plan, draft-only outreach, next actions, uncertainty disclosure, source trails, narrative sequences, prohibited overclaims, grounding audit checks, pairwise contrast, audience switchboard guidance, and repair actions; broader bios, through-line selection, weakness story, and richer audience-specific narratives remain incomplete.
- [~] Personal Digital Twin: private/local reasoning now covers work, goals, strengths, stale projects, skills, opportunities, deadlines, narratives, proof gaps, self-review reports, chief-of-staff briefs, and next actions.
- [~] Local-First Privacy Architecture: privacy levels, projection, redaction, approval workflow, local-only approval store, private briefing routes, public/private boundary, and audit log exist; broader encrypted private document storage and per-field approval UI remain incomplete.
- [~] Deployment And Runtime Truth: local runtime, package identity, inline build markers, git branch/ref state, configured production domains, local-vs-production disclosure, runtime fingerprints, volatile-vs-identity diffing, readiness scoring, route manifest attestation, private-gate attestation, refresh coverage, publish badge gating, and local receipts are tracked; CDN-level and remote deploy-provider identity remain incomplete.
- [~] Design Ambition: command-center density, section jumps, terminal shortcut strip, keyboard-visible focus, selected-state semantics, graph-mode pressed state, responsive overflow checks, and visual baselines now exist; broader premium art direction, full keyboard command palette, and all viewport/browser polish remain incomplete.
- [~] Research-Grade Evaluation: app evaluates truthfulness, evidence coverage, search quality, opportunity quality, recruiter/intent paths, accessibility, local performance, visual snapshots, mobile overflow, demos, claim freshness, privacy, opportunity relevance, maintenance, runtime truth, proof-quality benchmarks, project benchmark scores, graph integrity, graph normalization/render coverage, proof-trial guardrails, and maintainability checks; deeper narrative/technical-depth evaluations remain incomplete.

## Completed Wave 1

Wave 1: repair reproducibility and project metadata.

Rationale:

- `maygoals.md` wave generator starts with reproducibility and metadata.
- Existing tests and runtime were present but not runnable through documented package scripts.
- Missing referenced `main.js` and `favicon.svg` weakened runtime truth.
- A Playwright config is required for the existing E2E spec to run as written.
- The progress ledger is explicitly required by the execution contract.

## Evidence

- API contract test passed before edits: 6 tests, 1 suite, 0 failures.
- Missing files before edits: `package.json`, root lockfile, `README.md`, `playwright.config.*`, `main.js`, `favicon.svg`, `scripts/embed-runtime.mjs`.
- Added `package.json` with `start`, `check`, `build:inline`, `test`, `test:e2e`, and `verify`.
- Added `package-lock.json` via `npm install`.
- Added `README.md`, `playwright.config.mjs`, `scripts/embed-runtime.mjs`, `main.js`, and `favicon.svg`.
- `npm run verify` passed after the changes.
- `git status --short` now works after `git init -b main`.

## Completed Wave 2

Wave 2: extract portfolio data and add first claim/evidence/trust projection.

Rationale:

- `maygoals.md` requires a universal evidence graph, claim ledger, provenance/confidence, public trust console, proof-backed search, and terminal expansion.
- The previous app mixed source records and server behavior in `server.js`, so future evidence work needed a data boundary first.

Changes:

- Added `data/portfolio-data.js` as the data-only source for projects, archive notes, domains, internal checks, live demo checks, and profile.
- Added `data/evidence-model.js` with public-safe claim ledger generation, source material, evidence strength, privacy level, freshness score, confidence score, suggested repair, project evidence projection, and trust summary.
- Updated `server.js` to import data, build a claim ledger, enrich search results with evidence snippets/confidence/related claim IDs, and serve `/api/claims`, `/api/evidence/:slug`, and `/api/trust`.
- Expanded terminal commands with `claims`, `claims <slug>`, `verified`, `stale`, `demos`, `evidence <slug>`, `risks <slug>`, and `graph`.
- Updated API contract tests to cover claim/evidence/trust projections and new terminal commands.
- Updated `README.md` with the new endpoints.
- Updated `package.json` check script to syntax-check both data modules.

Evidence:

- `npm run verify` passed after the change.
- `test/api-contract.test.mjs` now verifies QAgent claim projection, QAgent evidence projection, trust counts, enriched search metadata, and terminal claim/evidence/risk commands.
- Live smoke checks on port 4181 confirmed `/api/claims?project=qagent`, `/api/evidence/qagent`, `/api/trust`, and terminal `evidence qagent`.

## Completed Wave 3

Wave 3: data validation gate.

Rationale:

- The evidence and claim ledger cannot become trustworthy if malformed project records, unsafe public projections, invalid URLs, impossible confidence scores, or sensitive strings can silently ship.

Changes:

- Added `scripts/validate-data.mjs`.
- Validator checks project shape, unique slugs, URLs, gradients, stack/tags/proof arrays, profile data, status check records, archive notes, claim IDs, public projection safety, privacy boundaries, confidence/freshness ranges, and sensitive-string patterns.
- `npm run check` now includes the validator.

Evidence:

- `npm run check` passed with `Validated 13 projects, 74 claims, 13 checks, 41 private references.`
- `npm run verify` passed after the validator was added.

## Completed Wave 4

Wave 4: public trust console.

Rationale:

- `/api/trust` should not remain hidden infrastructure; `maygoals.md` requires public trust indicators that make verification, stale/private boundaries, and evidence counts visible without oversharing.

Changes:

- Added a `Claim ledger` panel to the command center in `index.html`.
- Updated `command-center.mjs` to load `/api/trust` with the project/graph payloads and render claim counts plus strongest public-safe claims.
- Added trust console styles in `styles.css`.
- Extended Playwright E2E coverage to assert the trust metrics and strongest-claim list render.

Evidence:

- `npm run verify` passed after the UI change.
- Browser plugin rendered `http://127.0.0.1:4182/`; snapshot contained the `Claim ledger.` region; console health check reported 0 warnings/errors.

## Completed Wave 5

Wave 5: local-private cockpit boundary.

Rationale:

- `maygoals.md` asks for a private intelligence cockpit and local-first privacy architecture, but the repo must not expose fake or sensitive private data publicly.

Changes:

- Added `data/private-cockpit.js`, derived from public-safe claim/trust metadata.
- Added `/api/private/cockpit`, disabled by default and only available when `ENABLE_PRIVATE_COCKPIT=1` from localhost.
- Payload includes daily digest, claim approval queue, evidence repair queue, demo repair queue, project weakness map, prioritized projects, and next actions.
- Added validator coverage for private cockpit shape and sensitive-text checks.
- Added API contract coverage proving the private route is disabled by default.

Evidence:

- `npm run verify` passed after the private cockpit work.
- Live local enabled route returned `local-private-cockpit 12 13`.
- Live enabled route returned HTTP `200` with a 16305-byte JSON payload.

## Completed Wave 6

Wave 6: evidence-derived opportunity radar.

Rationale:

- `maygoals.md` asks for an opportunity engine, but fake postings, deadlines, grants, or application states would violate the truth rules. This wave creates the model and local evidence ranking without pretending live ingestion exists.

Changes:

- Added `data/opportunity-model.js`.
- Added `/api/opportunities`.
- Added terminal `opportunities` and `next` commands.
- Validator now checks opportunity shape, fit score range, missing proof, related proof, requirements, risks, next actions, sensitive text, and that deadlines remain `null` until real ingestion exists.
- API tests now cover the opportunity radar and terminal commands.
- README now lists `/api/opportunities`.

Evidence:

- `npm run verify` passed after the opportunity radar.
- Live `/api/opportunities` smoke returned `archetype-radar 6 true 3`, proving six opportunity archetypes, null deadline on the first result, and three next actions.
- Live terminal `opportunities` smoke returned five ranked lines containing fit scores.

## Completed Wave 7

Wave 7: evidence-backed case-study payloads.

Rationale:

- `maygoals.md` requires case studies compiled from evidence, not copied manually in five unrelated places.

Changes:

- Updated `caseStudy()` to include `sourceConfidence`, `evidencePacket`, and generated forms.
- Added source-backed claims and open proof gaps to the case-study section list.
- Generated five case-study forms: 30-second summary, 90-second recruiter brief, technical deep dive, proof-only audit, and weaknesses.
- API tests now assert the QAgent case study includes evidence packet, source confidence, proof-audit form, source-backed claims, and proof gaps.

Evidence:

- `npm run verify` passed after the case-study changes.
- Live QAgent case-study smoke returned `qagent 5 80 true`, proving five forms, confidence score 80, and source-backed claims section.

## Completed Wave 8

Wave 8: verification receipt persistence.

Rationale:

- `maygoals.md` requires verification receipts. Ephemeral `/api/status` responses were not enough because they left no durable trail.

Changes:

- Added `data/verification-receipts.js`.
- `/api/status` now appends a public-safe receipt with ID, timestamp, base URL, summary counts, and check summaries.
- Added `/api/status/history`.
- Added `var/` to `.gitignore` for generated runtime receipts.
- API tests now assert status receipt IDs and history.
- README now lists `/api/status/history`.

Evidence:

- `npm run verify` passed after receipt persistence.
- Live `/api/status` smoke returned `true 13 13`, proving receipt ID, summary total, and check count.
- Live `/api/status/history` smoke returned `4 13`, proving receipt history persisted and latest receipt summarized 13 checks.

## Completed Wave 9

Wave 9: semantic graph payload.

Rationale:

- The graph was still mostly project/skill/domain oriented. `maygoals.md` calls for claims, opportunities, and verification receipts to become graph entities.

Changes:

- `/api/graph` now includes top claim nodes with confidence/evidence/privacy metadata.
- `/api/graph` now includes opportunity nodes with fit scores and audience metadata.
- `/api/graph` includes recent verification receipt nodes when local receipt history exists.
- Added edges from projects to claims, projects to opportunities, and trust console to receipts.
- API tests now assert claim nodes, opportunity nodes, and opportunity edges.

Evidence:

- `npm run verify` passed after graph changes.
- Live semantic graph smoke returned `true true true`, proving claim nodes, opportunity nodes, and project-to-opportunity edges exist.

## Completed Wave 10

Wave 10: accessible semantic graph fallback.

Rationale:

- The WebGL constellation was visually useful but did not expose equivalent non-WebGL, text-first exploration for claims, opportunities, receipts, and relation trails.

Changes:

- `/api/graph` edges now carry `relation` and `explanation` fields.
- Added `#graph-fallback` under the graph readout.
- The fallback renders node-type counts, top claim nodes, top opportunity nodes, recent receipt nodes, and relation-trail buttons from the graph payload.
- Fallback buttons update the selected graph readout and active project without requiring canvas interaction.
- Added API tests for edge explanations and E2E tests for fallback rendering plus opportunity-node interaction.

Evidence:

- `npm run check` passed after the graph fallback work.
- `npm run verify` passed after the graph fallback work.
- E2E clicked the first opportunity fallback row and verified the graph readout switched to an `Opportunity route`.
- Browser fallback automation loaded `http://127.0.0.1:4183/`; `#graph-fallback` snapshot showed node counts and relation trails; console warnings/errors were 0.

## Completed Wave 11

Wave 11: filterable artifact wall.

Rationale:

- `maygoals.md` requires artifact-wall filtering by type, audience, year, proof strength, and privacy label. The prior preview wall showed generated cards only, with no way to inspect the proof surface by signal.

Changes:

- Added artifact wall filters for type, audience, year, proof strength, and privacy.
- Loaded `/api/claims` in the command-center runtime so proof-strength badges and filters use the full claim ledger, not only the top graph subset.
- Derived artifact metadata from project links, visibility, timeline, audience terms, and claim evidence strength.
- Artifact cards now show type, audience, year, proof, and privacy badges derived from project plus claim metadata.
- Removed the old 10-card cap so all 13 project artifacts participate.
- Added E2E coverage for filtering by repo, link-backed proof, public-safe private privacy, and reset.

Evidence:

- `npm run verify` initially caught the old 10-card preview cap; removed the cap so all 13 artifacts participate in filtering.
- `npm run verify` passed after the artifact filter work.
- Fallback browser automation loaded `http://127.0.0.1:4184/`; `#artifact-summary` showed `13 of 13 artifacts · All signals`; `.artifact-controls` exposed all five filter comboboxes and reset; browser console returned 0 warnings/errors.

## Completed Wave 12

Wave 12: artifact catalog and source tracing.

Rationale:

- `maygoals.md` asks for screenshots, replays, terminal transcripts, cards, and source tracing. Real screenshots/videos were not present in the repo, so this wave models available public-safe artifacts and explicit screenshot gaps instead of inventing media.

Changes:

- Added `data/artifact-model.js`.
- Added `/api/artifacts` with generated SVG previews, case-study API replays, terminal evidence replays, repo/live links, source traces, and screenshot-gap records.
- Validator now checks artifact catalog shape, available artifact counts, replay records, source traces, privacy, proof strength, and explicit screenshot gaps.
- Terminal command `artifacts` reports generated previews, API replays, terminal replays, and screenshot gaps.
- Artifact cards now render source trace rows from `/api/artifacts`.
- README now lists `/api/artifacts`.
- API and E2E tests now cover artifact catalog records and rendered source traces.

Evidence:

- `npm run verify` initially caught source-trace ordering that hid API replay rows behind repo links; fixed priority order and reran successfully.
- `npm run verify` passed after artifact catalog/source-trace changes; validator output included `51 artifacts`.
- Browser fallback automation loaded `http://127.0.0.1:4185/`; preview-wall snapshot showed `source trace` rows for every card; browser console returned 0 warnings/errors.

## Completed Wave 13

Wave 13: visitor intent paths.

Rationale:

- `/api/guide` previously returned ranked results, but `maygoals.md` requires supported visitor intents to receive best projects, proof paths, demos, risk disclosure, CTA, and time-boxed paths.

Changes:

- Added `data/intent-model.js` with recruiter, agent-infrastructure, civic-tech, research, and founder/collaborator intent profiles.
- Added `/api/intents` and `/api/intents/:id`.
- `/api/guide` now includes the selected `intentPath`.
- Added terminal command `intent [recruiter|agent-infra|civic-tech|research|founder]`.
- Rendered the selected intent path in the guide panel with timed steps, risk disclosure, and CTA.
- README now lists `/api/intents`.
- Validator and tests now cover intent path shape and rendered guide output.

Evidence:

- `npm run verify` passed after the intent-path work.
- Browser fallback automation loaded `http://127.0.0.1:4186/`; `#guide-answer` snapshot showed `Recruiter proof path`, 0-4 minute steps, risk disclosure, and CTA; browser console returned 0 warnings/errors.

## Completed Wave 14

Wave 14: maintenance report and stale-claim decay surface.

Rationale:

- `maygoals.md` requires stale claim detection and self-healing maintenance reports with decay diagnosis, severity, suggested fixes, and verification commands.

Changes:

- Added `data/maintenance-model.js`.
- Added `/api/maintenance`.
- Added terminal command `maintenance`.
- The maintenance report combines stale claims, needs-source claims, artifact gaps, private-reference review items, and latest status receipt failures.
- Each issue includes type, severity, project, detail, suggested fix, and verification command.
- Validator, API tests, and terminal command coverage now include maintenance reporting.
- README now lists `/api/maintenance`.

Evidence:

- `npm run verify` passed after the maintenance report work.
- Live `/api/maintenance` smoke returned `self-healing-maintenance-report 35 1 6`, proving mode, 35 issues, 1 high-severity issue, and 6 next safe actions.

## Completed Wave 15

Wave 15: runtime and deployment truth.

Rationale:

- `maygoals.md` requires local/production/build identity, domain health, and local-vs-production disclosure. Status checks existed, but runtime identity and build/git state were not exposed together.

Changes:

- Added `/api/runtime-truth`.
- Added terminal command `runtime`.
- Runtime truth includes environment, base URL, Node version, PID, port, package name/version, inline runtime marker status, git branch/ref/commit state, configured production domains, and local-vs-hosted differences.
- API tests and terminal command coverage now include runtime truth.
- README now lists `/api/runtime-truth`.

Evidence:

- `npm run verify` passed after runtime truth work.
- Live `/api/runtime-truth` smoke returned `runtime-truth local rishabh-personal-command-center true unborn-or-missing-ref 4`, proving local environment, package identity, inline bundle marker, current unborn/missing-ref git state, and four tracked domains.

## Completed Wave 16

Wave 16: privacy approval workflow and local-first private store.

Rationale:

- `maygoals.md` requires private artifacts to need explicit approval before public projection, and sensitive/private operational intelligence to stay in a local-first store excluded from public routes.

Changes:

- Added `data/privacy-approval.js`.
- Public artifact records now carry `approvalRequired` and `publicProjection` state.
- Added local-only `/api/private/approvals`, disabled by default and available only with `ENABLE_PRIVATE_COCKPIT=1` from localhost.
- Added local ignored store `var/private-approval-store.json` with approval records and audit log.
- Added POST workflow for `approved`, `rejected`, and `withheld` decisions.
- Private cockpit payload now includes `privacyApprovalAudit`.
- Added terminal command `approvals`.
- Validator, API contract tests, package checks, and README now cover the approval audit.

Evidence:

- `npm run check` passed after privacy approval changes.
- `npm run verify` passed after privacy approval changes.
- Public server tests verify `/api/private/approvals` returns 404 without the local private gate.
- Local enabled smoke with `ENABLE_PRIVATE_COCKPIT=1` returned `local-privacy-approval-audit 72 claim 1 1 true`, proving 72 pending candidates, a withheld local decision, cockpit audit visibility, and local-only storage.

## Completed Wave 17

Wave 17: safe evidence refresh job and receipts.

Rationale:

- `maygoals.md` requires automated evidence refresh jobs that update receipts without uncontrolled side effects.

Changes:

- Added `data/evidence-refresh.js`.
- Added `scripts/evidence-refresh.mjs`.
- Added package command `npm run refresh:evidence`.
- Added `/api/evidence-refresh/plan` and `/api/evidence-refresh/history`.
- Added terminal command `refresh`.
- Refresh runner starts a temporary local server, checks public-safe endpoints, writes `var/evidence-refresh-receipts.json`, and does not publish, deploy, approve private artifacts, or contact users.
- README, API tests, package checks, and terminal tests now cover the refresh plan/history surface.

Evidence:

- `npm run refresh:evidence` passed and wrote `refresh-1779867545068 6/6 passed`.
- Receipt readback returned `1 refresh-1779867545068 6 6`.
- `npm run verify` passed after the refresh job work.

## Completed Wave 18

Wave 18: scripted accessibility audit.

- Added package command `npm run audit:a11y`.
- Added `/api/accessibility-audit/plan` and `/api/accessibility-audit/history`.
- Added terminal command `a11y`.

- README, API tests, package checks, and terminal tests now cover the accessibility audit surface.

## Completed Wave 20

Wave 20: deterministic live proof trial descriptors and sandbox guardrails.

Rationale:

- `maygoals.md` asks for live proof trials and sandboxed demo runners. This repo does not have credentials, external demo fixtures, or production-safe remote runner infrastructure, so the feasible truthful step is deterministic local replay with explicit guardrails and machine-checkable assertions.

Changes:

- Tightened `data/proof-trials.js` into a `safe-live-proof-trials` catalog with execution boundary, summary counts, per-project public surfaces, sandbox metadata, read-only steps, assertions, approval-gate metadata, and result checks.
- Added `/api/proof-trials` and `/api/proof-trials/:slug`.
- Added terminal command `trial [slug]`.
- Validator now checks every trial is deterministic, read-only, credential-free, non-writing, privacy-gated, and free of sensitive text.
- API contract tests now cover the proof-trials catalog, the QAgent proof trial, terminal `trial qagent`, and 404 behavior for unknown proof trials.
- README now lists the proof-trial endpoints.

Evidence:

- `npm run check` passed after proof-trial wiring.
- `npm run verify` passed after proof-trial wiring: check, inline build, 9 API tests, and 2 E2E tests.
- Local smoke on `http://127.0.0.1:4191` returned:
  - `safe-live-proof-trials 13 0`
  - `qagent true 7 none forbidden`
  - `QAgent local evidence replay :: deterministic-local-replay :: passed`
  The smoke server was stopped afterward.

## Completed Wave 21

Wave 21: generated recruiter/professor/founder packets with uncertainty.

Rationale:

- `maygoals.md` requires generated recruiter/professor/founder packets and narrative intelligence that stays grounded in evidence. The safe implementation is a public-safe packet generator that composes existing claim, artifact, intent, opportunity, and trust data while disclosing uncertainty and forbidding automatic outreach.

Changes:

- Added `data/audience-packets.js` with `evidence-audience-packets` for recruiter, professor, and founder audiences.
- Each packet includes decision question, thesis, short pitch, long pitch, recommended project order, evidence briefs, proof plan, draft-only outreach, uncertainty disclosure, and next actions.
- Added `/api/packets` and `/api/packets/:audience`.
- Added terminal command `packet [recruiter|professor|founder]` and routed `fit professor` / `fit founder` through packet evidence order.
- Validator now checks packet selection aliases, uncertainty, caveats, draft-only send policy, evidence briefs, proof plans, artifacts, and sensitive-text safety.
- API contract tests now cover packet endpoints, uncertainty disclosure, draft-only policy, aliases, unknown packet 404, and new terminal commands.
- README and package checks now include the audience-packet surface.

Evidence:

- `npm run check` passed after packet wiring.
- `npm run verify` passed after packet wiring: check, inline build, 9 API tests, and 2 E2E tests.
- Local smoke on `http://127.0.0.1:4192` returned:
  - `evidence-audience-packets 3 recruiter,professor,founder`
  - `professor 30 5 13`
  - `Founder or collaborator packet :: confidence 42/100 (insufficient)`
  The low packet confidence is intentional evidence of uncertainty disclosure, not a failure. The smoke server was stopped afterward.

## Completed Wave 22

Wave 22: visual regression and local screenshot baselines.

Rationale:

- `maygoals.md` requires screenshots or snapshot comparisons for key UI flows. Existing E2E tests checked behavior and mobile overflow, but not visual drift.

Changes:

- Added `scripts/visual-regression.mjs`, which starts a temporary local server, captures stable element screenshots with Playwright, stores local ignored screenshots/baselines under `var/`, compares image/text hashes, and records reports.
- Added package command `npm run audit:visual`.
- Added `/api/visual-regression/plan` and `/api/visual-regression/history`.
- Added terminal command `visuals`.
- Validator, API contract tests, package checks, and README now cover the visual-regression surface.
- Fixed the visual audit selector for QAgent case study opening after Playwright correctly reported that `[data-project='qagent']` matched multiple controls.

- `npm run verify` passed after visual-regression work: check, inline build, 9 API tests, and 2 E2E tests.

## Completed Wave 27

Wave 27: skill gap maps.

Rationale:

- `maygoals.md` requires skills to distinguish proven, claimed, weak, and missing proof. Existing graph skill nodes were labels only; they did not evaluate evidence quality.

Changes:

- Added `data/skill-map.js` with `public-skill-gap-map`.
- Skills are derived from project tags and stack entries, then classified by skill-specific claim text, public-safe artifacts, private references, stale claims, and artifact gaps.
- Added `/api/skill-gaps` and `/api/skill-gaps/:skill`.
- Added terminal command `skills [skill]`.
- Validator checks skill statuses, proof buckets, evidence counts, missing-proof arrays, improvement actions, and sensitive-text safety.
- API contract tests cover skill endpoints, terminal commands, and unknown skill 404 behavior.
- README and package checks now include the skill gap surface.
- The first implementation over-counted project-level evidence as skill evidence; it was tightened so a skill needs skill-specific claim text before it can avoid the `missing-proof` bucket.

Evidence:

- `npm run verify` passed after skill gap wiring: check, inline build, 9 API tests, and 2 E2E tests.

## Completed Wave 28

Wave 28: contradiction detection and quarantine.

Rationale:

- `maygoals.md` requires conflicting claims to be detected and quarantined. The live data currently has no known conflicts, so the implementation needed both a live report and synthetic validation to prove the quarantine path works.

Changes:

- Added `data/contradiction-model.js` with `claim-contradiction-report`.
- Rules detect private/public projection conflicts, needs-source/source-backed verification conflicts, duplicate project URLs, and simple opposing claim text.
- Added quarantine entries that exclude affected claims from strongest/public proof ordering until review.
- Added `/api/contradictions`.
- Added terminal command `contradictions`.
- Validator injects synthetic privacy, verification, and opposing-text conflicts to prove detection and quarantine.
- API contract tests also include a synthetic privacy conflict and verify quarantine membership.
- README and package checks now include the contradiction surface.

Evidence:

- `npm run check` passed after contradiction wiring.
- `npm run verify` passed after contradiction wiring: check, inline build, 9 API tests, and 2 E2E tests.
- Live smoke on `http://127.0.0.1:4199` returned:
  - `claim-contradiction-report 0 0 4`
  - `claim-contradiction-report :: 0 conflict(s), 0 quarantined claim(s), 0 high severity.`
  The smoke server was stopped afterward.

## Completed Wave 31

Wave 31: derived Wave 41, evidence graph quality.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 41 asked to normalize evidence graph depth. The existing graph exposed nodes and edges, but it did not prove relationship integrity.

- Added `/api/graph-quality`.
- Added terminal command `graph-quality`.
- Validator checks graph-quality behavior against a complete validation graph.
- API contract tests now cover graph-quality endpoint and terminal command.
- Fixed a real graph defect: `Bluetooth` and `bluetooth` tags normalized to the same `skill-bluetooth` node id, so graph node generation now dedupes by normalized id.
- README and package checks now include graph quality.

## Completed Wave 35

Wave 35: derived Wave 45, runtime truth fingerprint/diff depth.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 45 asked for runtime-truth fingerprinting, verification, diffing, recording, or explanation. The app exposed current runtime truth, but it did not preserve a fingerprint trail or separate stable identity drift from volatile local restart noise.

Changes:

- Added `data/runtime-truth.js` with a runtime fingerprint report, readiness checks, stable identity hash, volatile hash, previous-receipt diffing, and local receipt persistence under ignored `var/runtime-truth-receipts.json`.
- Added `scripts/runtime-truth.mjs` and package command `npm run record:runtime`.
- Added `/api/runtime-truth/plan`, `/api/runtime-truth/fingerprint`, and `/api/runtime-truth/history`.
- Added terminal command `runtime-diff` and enriched `runtime` output with readiness and identity hash.
- Included the fingerprint endpoint in the safe evidence refresh plan.
- Validator checks runtime-truth plans, fingerprints, readiness, diffs, and sensitive-text safety.
- API contract tests cover the new plan, fingerprint, history, and terminal command.
- README and package checks now include runtime fingerprinting.

Evidence:

- `npm run check` passed after runtime fingerprint wiring.
- `npm run record:runtime` passed twice. The latest receipt reported `runtime-mpnv6y4e readiness 91/100; 3 runtime change(s); wrote var/runtime-truth-receipts.json`.
- `npm run verify` passed after runtime fingerprint wiring: check, inline build, 9 API tests, and 2 E2E tests.
- Live smoke on `http://127.0.0.1:4207` returned:
  - `runtime-truth-fingerprint 91 high 3 3`
  - `b697cae7f6bf633b 9317aa9f4fc56620`
  - `runtime-truth-fingerprint-plan npm run record:runtime var/runtime-truth-receipts.json`
  - `runtime-diff` reported previous receipt `runtime-mpnv6y4e` and explained that only volatile runtime fields changed.
  The smoke server was stopped afterward.

## Completed Wave 36

Wave 36: derived Wave 46, design-system polish and responsive command-center density.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 46 asked for design ambition polish, compression, navigation, adaptation, or stabilization. Rendered inspection found the command center wasting desktop vertical space because the search panel stretched to match the graph panel height.

Changes:

- Added command-center section jump links for Search, Graph, Cases, and Artifacts.
- Tightened the command grid so panels keep their natural height instead of stretching across rows.
- Added keyboard-visible focus styling for command-center controls.
- Added selected-project `aria-current` and graph-mode `aria-pressed` state updates.
- Normalized command-center letter spacing to avoid cramped negative tracking in compact tool surfaces.
- Added explicit visual baseline acceptance via `ACCEPT_VISUAL_CHANGES=1 npm run audit:visual`.
- E2E coverage now checks the command jump, selected project state, and graph mode pressed state.
- README documents the visual acceptance command.

Evidence:

- Browser plugin was attempted first and failed with `Browser is not available: iab`; validation used the Playwright fallback required by the frontend testing workflow.
- Pre-change Playwright inspection found no horizontal overflow, but the desktop search panel stretched to graph height.
- Post-change live smoke on `http://127.0.0.1:4208` confirmed no app errors, no horizontal overflow, command jump hash `#command-graph`, Rank mode `aria-pressed="true"`, and rendered search results for `runtime proof`.
- `npm run check` passed after the layout and visual-audit changes.
- `npm run verify` passed after Wave 36: check, inline build, 9 API tests, and 2 E2E tests.
- Initial `npm run audit:visual` failed intentionally with `0/4 passed; 4 changed`.
- `ACCEPT_VISUAL_CHANGES=1 npm run audit:visual` accepted the new local baselines with `4/4 passed; 4 baseline(s) updated`.
- Follow-up plain `npm run audit:visual` passed with `4/4 passed; 0 changed`.
- The smoke server was stopped afterward.

## Completed Wave 42

- Each package includes readiness score/band, selected packet confidence, tracking boundary, evidence bundle, requirement coverage, de-risking checklist, risk register, blockers, source trace, execution plan, decision gate, verification command, and next action.

- Validator, API contract tests, package checks, README, and terminal command coverage now include opportunity packages.
- Fixed a proof-trial assumption exposed by validation: package scoring now treats proof-trial catalog entries as safe replay descriptors instead of claiming executed trial results.

## Completed Wave 47

Wave 47: derived Wave 57, evidence graph normalization scoreboard.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 57 asked to score evidence graph depth. The graph already had quality checks and crosslinks, but it still could not summarize which modeled entities were actually rendered, which relationships were only canonical, which claims/links should be quarantined, or what graph repair would increase trust fastest.

Changes:

- Added `data/graph-scoreboard.js` with `evidence-graph-normalization-scoreboard`.
- The scoreboard normalizes public-safe graph, claim, artifact, opportunity, narrative, audience-packet, weakness, skill-gap, maintenance, contradiction, and crosslink data into 20 modeled entity types.
- It scores canonical entity coverage, canonical crosslink coverage, rendered crosslink coverage, structural integrity, quarantine pressure, and repair actionability.
- It exposes quarantine candidates for weak/stale/private rendered claims, graph-quality failures, contradictions, and low-confidence links without deleting source records.
- `/api/graph` now includes artifact, narrative, maintenance, and weakness nodes plus relation explanations for project artifacts, maintenance risks, weakness maps, and grounded narrative links.
- Added `/api/graph-scoreboard`.
- Added terminal command `graph-scoreboard` with aliases `graph-normalize` and `graph-score`.
- Added validator coverage, API contract coverage, package syntax coverage, README endpoint coverage, evidence refresh coverage, and runtime route-manifest coverage.

Wave 50: derived Wave 60, opportunity engine quality scoring.

## Completed Wave 51

Wave 51: derived Wave 61, runtime surface diff receipts.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 61 asked to improve runtime truth with a local or deploy-aware truth check, receipt, API surface, or terminal explanation. The runtime truth layer could fingerprint identity and compare prior receipts, but it did not yet prove that the declared route manifest matched a locally reachable public/static surface or that private routes stayed hidden by default.

Changes:

- Added `data/runtime-surface.js` with a runtime surface diff plan, route-manifest probe builder, latest receipt projection, receipt store, and report builder.
- Added `scripts/runtime-surface.mjs`, exposed as `npm run record:runtime-surface`, which starts a temporary local server, probes declared public API routes, static routes, and private-gated routes, and writes `var/runtime-surface-receipts.json`.
- Added `/api/runtime-surface/plan`, `/api/runtime-surface`, `/api/runtime-surface/latest`, and `/api/runtime-surface/history`.
- Added terminal command `runtime-surface` with aliases `surface-diff` and `route-surface`.
- Expanded runtime attestation so package/route checks now require the runtime-surface recorder and expose a `runtime-surface-diff` attestation.
- Added runtime-surface latest coverage to the safe evidence refresh plan.
- Updated validator coverage, API contract coverage, package syntax coverage, README command/endpoint docs, and runtime route manifest declarations.

## Completed Wave 52

Wave 52: derived Wave 62, dense keyboard-first proof UI.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 62 asked to improve dense, accessible, mobile-safe, keyboard-first UI without hiding uncertainty. The command center exposed many proof systems, but the first command surface still made users inspect separate panels or terminal commands to understand proof health, runtime surface freshness, graph coverage, and opportunity readiness.

Changes:

- Added a compact `#proof-ribbon` inside the command shell.
- The ribbon is fed by real public-safe APIs: trust counts, graph scoreboard, runtime surface latest receipt, and opportunity quality evaluation.
- The ribbon intentionally exposes weak spots instead of smoothing them over: `13 need source`, `36% rendered refs`, `0/6` ready opportunity packages, and `43 missing proof`.
- Added `runtime-surface` to the terminal shortcut buttons so the newly implemented route-surface receipt is one click away in the UI.
- Added arrow-key navigation for the project index: `ArrowDown`, `ArrowUp`, `Home`, and `End` move through projects, update the selected case study, and preserve focus after the async case-study/graph sync.
- Added responsive ribbon styling with fixed grid constraints, ellipsis protection, and no mobile horizontal overflow.
- Expanded Playwright E2E coverage for the proof ribbon, the runtime-surface shortcut, and keyboard project navigation.

Evidence:

- `npm run check` passed after the first Wave 52 implementation: `Validated 13 projects, 74 claims, 64 artifacts, 13 checks, 41 private references.`
- Browser plugin path was attempted first for rendered QA but failed because the in-app browser target `iab` was unavailable in this runtime. Fallback used regular Playwright against `http://127.0.0.1:4224`.
- Initial rendered fallback found the ribbon and terminal shortcut, but it exposed two useful issues:
  - opportunity detail used the wrong summary field and displayed `0 blockers`; fixed to `43 missing proof`
  - async case-study graph sync dropped keyboard focus after arrow navigation; fixed by preserving focus through `setGraphReadout`
- Final rendered Playwright smoke on `http://127.0.0.1:4224` returned:
  - desktop title `Rishabh Bansal — selected works`
  - not blank: `Search the proof graph.`
  - ribbon `CLAIMS 53 13 NEED SOURCE GRAPH 71/100 36% RENDERED REFS RUNTIME 100/100 80/80 PROBES OPPORTUNITIES 0/6 43 MISSING PROOF`
  - no framework overlay: `true`
  - keyboard focus after `ArrowDown`: `flowpr`
  - case-study heading after keyboard move: `FlowPR`
  - terminal shortcut output contained `runtime-surface-diff`
  - desktop and mobile overflow checks: `true`
  - screenshots saved outside repo:
    - `/tmp/personal-wave52-desktop.png`
    - `/tmp/personal-wave52-mobile.png`
  - only known WebGL driver `ReadPixels` performance warnings appeared
- `npm run verify` passed after Wave 52: check, inline build with `532916 bytes`, 9 API tests, and 2 Playwright E2E tests.
- Follow-up listener check for ports 4224 and 4173 returned no listener.

  - trust summary
  - graph scoreboard
  - runtime surface latest receipt
  - opportunity quality
  - proof quality
  - search quality
  - accessibility audit reports
  - performance budget reports
  - visual regression reports
  - refresh plan
  - route manifest
- Added control benchmarks for proof ribbon, keyboard project navigation, runtime-surface shortcut, scripted accessibility, performance budget, and visual baseline.
- Added `/api/evaluation/usability`.
- Added terminal command `usability-quality` with aliases `usability-eval` and `ux-quality`.
- Added the usability endpoint to the runtime route manifest and safe evidence refresh plan.
- Added validator coverage, API contract coverage, package syntax coverage, README endpoint docs, and refresh summary support.

Wave 54: derived Wave 64, narrative objection pressure tests.

Rationale:

The app had grounded narratives and audience contrast, but it did not yet pressure-test those narratives against skeptical reader objections or produce public-safe answers, caveats, evidence IDs, and repair actions for each audience.

  - private-reference boundaries
  - overclaim control
  - action/readiness limits

- Added audience-level disclosure checklists and safest-use guidance.
- Added `/api/narrative-objections` and `/api/narrative-objections/:audience`.
- Added terminal command `narrative-objections [audience]` with aliases `objections` and `pressure-test`.
- Added the objection report to the runtime route manifest and safe evidence refresh plan.
- Added validator coverage, API contract coverage, package syntax coverage, README endpoint docs, and refresh summary support.

  - public-only runtime truth plan
  - private runtime-surface probe coverage
  - latest private-gate surface receipt
  - runtime attestation private-gate coverage
  - source-level gate signals
  - README private-boundary disclosure
  - package script coverage for verification

- Added validator coverage, API contract coverage, package syntax coverage, README endpoint docs, and refresh summary support.

## Completed Wave 59

Wave 59: derived Wave 68, proof-backed opportunity board.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 68 asked to deepen the opportunity engine through readiness scoring, manual-use gates, and proof-backed bundles. The app already had an archetype radar, opportunity packages, and opportunity quality evaluation, but it did not have one operating board that sorted packages into review gates, grouped them by audience lane, exposed blocker queues, and kept the no-live-application boundary visible.

Changes:

- Added `data/opportunity-board.js` with `proof-backed-opportunity-board`.
- Added public endpoint `/api/opportunity-board`.
- Added terminal command `opportunity-board` with aliases `opportunity-brief` and `opportunity-readiness`.
- The board now exposes:
  - three manual-use gates: `ready-for-manual-review`, `proof-repair-required`, and `blocked-until-proof`
  - proof bundles for every opportunity package
  - a prioritized blocker queue with local-only repair boundaries
  - audience lanes for agent infrastructure, research/professor review, civic/public-interest, founder/demo, and devtools/open-source review
  - operating rules that forbid automatic sending, submissions, scheduling, third-party writes, and fake live posting/application status
  - board integrity checks for manual policy, gate assignment, proof-bundle depth, blocker actionability, audience-lane coverage, route manifest coverage, and refresh coverage
- Added the board endpoint to the runtime route manifest and safe evidence refresh plan.
- Added validator coverage, API contract coverage, package syntax coverage, README endpoint docs, refresh summary support, and terminal command coverage.

## Completed Wave 60

Wave 60: derived Wave 69, runtime truth reconciliation.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 69 asked to record runtime truth phase 4. The app already had runtime truth fingerprints, runtime surface receipts, evidence refresh receipts, attestation, and public/private boundary reports, but there was no single public-safe ledger proving that those receipts were fresh relative to the current route manifest and refresh plan.

Changes:

- Added `data/runtime-reconciliation.js` with `runtime-truth-reconciliation`.
- Added `/api/runtime-reconciliation`.
- Added terminal command `runtime-reconciliation` with aliases `runtime-proof` and `truth-ledger`.
- The reconciliation report now compares:
  - latest runtime truth receipt identity hash versus the current runtime identity hash
  - latest runtime surface receipt probe count and private-gate coverage versus the current route manifest
  - latest evidence refresh receipt endpoint count versus the current refresh plan
  - runtime attestation score, boundary score, public-safe plan boundaries, truth route coverage, reconciliation refresh coverage, and recorder script coverage
- Added a receipt matrix for runtime truth, runtime surface, and evidence refresh.
- Added a drift matrix for runtime identity, route surface, and evidence refresh endpoint coverage.
- Added the reconciliation endpoint to the runtime route manifest and safe evidence refresh plan.
- Added validator coverage, API contract coverage, package syntax coverage, README endpoint docs, refresh summary support, and terminal command coverage.

## Completed Wave 61

Wave 61: derived Wave 70, dense command-center UI phase 4.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 70 asked to push the visible product experience forward without hiding uncertainty. The command center already had search, graph, case studies, artifacts, terminal commands, and status checks, but the receipt/gate state lived mostly in APIs and terminal output. The app needed a first-class, keyboard-reachable ledger that surfaced runtime receipts, opportunity blockers, graph quality, and proof-quality uncertainty directly inside the main UI.

Changes:

- Added a `Ledger` jump target to the command-center nav and a new `#command-ledger` panel titled `Receipts, gates, and unresolved proof.`
- Added `#truth-ledger` rendering in `command-center.mjs`, backed by `/api/runtime-reconciliation` and `/api/opportunity-board`.
- The ledger now exposes:
  - runtime reconciliation score, passing check count, and stale-receipt count
  - latest runtime-surface receipt freshness
  - opportunity-gate readiness and missing-proof blockers
  - graph normalization score and repair guidance
  - opportunity quality score and proof-routing state
- Added ledger action buttons that populate and run terminal commands, including `runtime-reconciliation`.
- Added visible terminal shortcuts for `runtime-reconciliation` and `opportunity-board`.
- Fixed a rendered-UI bug where inline runtime builds could leave `.reveal` sections opacity-zero because `main.js` was not running; the command-center runtime now fails open to visible content before attaching observers.
- Added responsive ledger CSS and mobile grid resets so the new panel stacks without horizontal overflow.
- Expanded Playwright E2E coverage to assert the ledger, terminal shortcuts, ledger-to-terminal interaction, and mobile rendering.
- Tightened the desktop E2E by removing a duplicate terminal run and setting a `60_000ms` timeout for the intentionally broad desktop smoke.

Evidence:

- Browser plugin path was attempted for rendered QA, but invocation failed with `Browser is not available: iab`; regular Playwright was used as the recorded fallback.
- `npm run check` passed after the UI changes: `Validated 13 projects, 74 claims, 64 artifacts, 13 checks, 41 private references.`
- `npm run build:inline` passed and embedded `535979 bytes` from `command-center.mjs` into `index.html`.
- Fresh local receipts were recorded after the inline runtime changed:
  - `npm run record:runtime` produced `runtime-mpo3v1r1 readiness 91/100; 5 runtime change(s); wrote var/runtime-truth-receipts.json`
  - `npm run refresh:evidence` produced `refresh-1779888847245 19/19 passed; wrote var/evidence-refresh-receipts.json`
- Manual Playwright fallback QA on `http://127.0.0.1:4234/?wave61=final` confirmed:
  - desktop shell visible, ledger visible, runtime and opportunity rows present
  - ledger showed fresh receipt state with `0` stale receipts
  - ledger `runtime-reconciliation` action populated and ran the terminal command
  - desktop and mobile both had no horizontal overflow
  - mobile ledger stacked cleanly without overlap
  - screenshots were saved at `/tmp/personal-ledger-desktop-final.png` and `/tmp/personal-ledger-mobile-fixed.png`
  - only observed console warnings were WebGL readback warnings from screenshot/canvas inspection, not app errors.
- Post-verify live reconciliation smoke on `http://127.0.0.1:4234/api/runtime-reconciliation` returned:
  - `100 | high | 8/8 | 0`
  - receipts `runtime-truth:fresh:runtime-mpo3v1r1 runtime-surface:fresh:surface-mpo3emme evidence-refresh:fresh:refresh-1779888847245`
  - surface `91/91`, refresh `19/19`, all checks passing.
- `npm run test:e2e` passed after the E2E timeout fix: 2 tests, desktop `41.0s`, mobile `2.7s`.
- `npm run verify` passed after Wave 61: check, inline build with `535979 bytes`, 9 API tests, and 2 Playwright E2E tests.
  The smoke server was stopped afterward and a listener check for ports 4234 and 4173 returned no listener.

## Completed Wave 62

Wave 62: derived Wave 71, research-grade evaluation stress phase 4.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 71 asked for a repeatable evaluation stress test across truthfulness, usability, performance, accessibility, or proof quality. The app already had separate proof, search, opportunity, usability, runtime, graph, and receipt evaluations, but no adversarial suite that forced them to agree under pressure. This wave added a public-safe stress report that rewards visible uncertainty, detects stale runtime receipts, and fails if the evaluation surface is not route/refresh/script discoverable.

Changes:

- Added `data/research-evaluation-stress.js` with `research-grade-evaluation-stress-suite`.
- Added public routes:
  - `/api/evaluation/research-stress`
  - `/api/evaluation/research-stress/plan`
  - `/api/evaluation/research-stress/history`
- Added `scripts/research-evaluation-stress.mjs` and `npm run stress:evaluation`, which starts a temporary local server, reads the public stress endpoint, and writes local receipts to `var/research-evaluation-stress-receipts.json`.
- Added terminal command `research-stress` with aliases `evaluation-stress` and `stress-eval`.
- Added the visible terminal shortcut `research-stress`.
- Added the stress endpoint to the runtime route manifest and safe evidence refresh plan.
- Added evidence-refresh summary support, README command/endpoint docs, package syntax coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, terminal command coverage, and Playwright shortcut coverage.
- The stress suite now evaluates seven adversarial scenarios:
  - unsupported claim pressure
  - retrieval proof pressure
  - manual opportunity gate pressure
  - runtime drift pressure
  - graph uncertainty pressure
  - verification receipt pressure
  - route and refresh coverage pressure

Evidence:

- `npm run check` passed after the initial implementation: `Validated 13 projects, 74 claims, 64 artifacts, 13 checks, 41 private references.`
- First local smoke on `http://127.0.0.1:4235/api/evaluation/research-stress` intentionally exposed stale receipts after the route set changed:
  - stress `83 medium 6/7`
  - failing scenario `runtime-drift-pressure`
  - route covered `true`
  - refresh covered `true`
  - terminal `research-stress` began with `research-grade-evaluation-stress-suite :: 83/100 medium; 6/7 scenario(s) passing.`
- After rebuilding and refreshing receipts:

  - `npm run record:runtime` produced `runtime-mpo4mt8x readiness 91/100; 4 runtime change(s); wrote var/runtime-truth-receipts.json`
  - `npm run record:runtime-surface` produced `surface-mpo4n1q3 94/94 passed; 9/9 private gate probe(s); wrote var/runtime-surface-receipts.json`
  - `npm run refresh:evidence` produced `refresh-1779890159240 20/20 passed; wrote var/evidence-refresh-receipts.json`
  - `npm run stress:evaluation` produced `research-stress-mpo4nezd 7/7 scenario(s) passed; score 97/100; wrote var/research-evaluation-stress-receipts.json`
- Second local smoke on `http://127.0.0.1:4235` returned:
  - stress `97:high:7/7:0`
  - latest stress receipt `research-stress-mpo4nezd`
  - runtime surface `94/94`

- `npm run verify` passed after Wave 62: check, inline build with `535979 bytes`, 9 API tests, and 2 Playwright E2E tests.

## Completed Wave 63

Wave 63: derived Wave 72, narrative intelligence tailoring phase 4.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 72 asked to generate or validate narrative output from evidence with uncertainty and repair guidance. The app already had audience packets, grounded narratives, narrative contrast, and objection pressure tests, but it did not have a tailor that produced audience-specific narrative variants while carrying claim IDs, artifact IDs, caveats, objection answers, repair guidance, and manual-use boundaries.

Changes:

- Added `data/narrative-tailor.js` with `evidence-backed-narrative-tailor`.
- Added public routes:
  - `/api/narrative-tailor`
  - `/api/narrative-tailor/:audience`
  - `/api/narrative-tailor/plan`
  - `/api/narrative-tailor/history`
- Added `scripts/narrative-tailor.mjs` and `npm run tailor:narratives`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/narrative-tailor`, and writes receipts to `var/narrative-tailor-receipts.json`.
- Added terminal command `narrative-tailor` with aliases `tailor-narrative` and `tailor`.
- Added the visible terminal shortcut `narrative-tailor`.
- Added the tailor endpoint to the runtime route manifest and safe evidence refresh plan.
- Added README docs, package syntax coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, evidence refresh summary support, terminal command coverage, and Playwright shortcut coverage.
- The tailor now generates three variants for each audience:
  - `proof-first`
  - `caveat-forward`
  - `ask-ready-draft`
- Every variant carries claim IDs, artifact IDs, caveats, an objection answer, repair guidance, prohibited-overclaim guards, a manual-use boundary, and a verification command.

## Completed Wave 64

Wave 64: derived Wave 73, evidence graph projection guard phase 5.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 73 asked for a verifiable evidence-graph relationship, confidence rule, or projection guard. The app now has tailored narrative variants with claim and artifact references, but those relationships should not silently imply rendered graph completeness or external readiness. This wave added a public-safe graph projection guard that derives relationships from tailored narratives, checks source resolution, classifies rendered versus quarantined relationships, and enforces low-confidence/manual-use boundaries.

Changes:

- Added `data/graph-projection-guard.js` with `evidence-graph-projection-guard`.
- Added public route `/api/graph-projection-guard`.
- Added terminal command `graph-guard` with aliases `projection-guard` and `graph-projection`.
- Added the visible terminal shortcut `graph-guard`.
- Added the graph guard endpoint to the runtime route manifest and safe evidence refresh plan.
- Added README docs, package syntax coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, evidence-refresh summary support, terminal command coverage, and Playwright shortcut coverage.
- The guard now derives and validates relationships:
  - `tailors-narrative`
  - `tailored-narrative-uses-claim`
  - `tailored-narrative-cites-artifact`
- Projection rules now distinguish:
  - rendered relationships where both graph nodes exist
  - quarantined inspection-only relationships where source data is valid but graph nodes are not rendered
  - draft-only low-confidence tailored variants
  - private/public-safe references that must not be projected as externally ready

Evidence:

- `npm run check` passed after implementation: `Validated 13 projects, 74 claims, 64 artifacts, 13 checks, 41 private references.`
- First live smoke on `http://127.0.0.1:4237` returned:
  - guard `100 high 8/8 81 relationships, 0 rendered, 81 quarantined, 0 source missing`
  - checks `relationship-depth:true source-resolution:true rendered-or-quarantined:true quarantine-repair-guidance:true low-confidence-draft-only:true private-public-safe:true route-manifest:true refresh-plan:true`
  - first relations `tailors-narrative tailored-narrative-uses-claim tailored-narrative-uses-claim`
  - terminal `graph-guard` began with `evidence-graph-projection-guard :: 100/100 high; 8/8 guard(s) passing.`
  - runtime reconciliation was stale at `56` with `3` stale receipt kinds, correctly reflecting the route-set change before receipt refresh.
- After refreshing receipts:
  - `npm run build:inline` embedded `535979 bytes` from `command-center.mjs` into `index.html`

  - `npm run record:runtime-surface` produced `surface-mpo5lgfb 99/99 passed; 9/9 private gate probe(s); wrote var/runtime-surface-receipts.json`
  - `npm run refresh:evidence` produced `refresh-1779891770853 22/22 passed; wrote var/evidence-refresh-receipts.json`
  - `npm run stress:evaluation` produced `research-stress-mpo5lyfr 7/7 scenario(s) passed; score 97/100; wrote var/research-evaluation-stress-receipts.json`
  - `npm run tailor:narratives` produced `narrative-tailor-mpo5m4xf 6/6 check(s) passed; 9 variant(s); score 97/100; wrote var/narrative-tailor-receipts.json`
- Post-refresh smoke on `http://127.0.0.1:4237` returned:

  - `reconciliation:100:8/8:0`
  - `refresh:22:true`

## Completed Wave 66

Wave 66: derived Wave 75, private chief-of-staff schedule phase 5.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 75 asked to improve local/private scheduling without exposing private documents or sending anything automatically. The app already had private next actions, task tracking, review sessions, briefing drafts, outreach drafts, approvals, and chief-of-staff readiness, but it did not have a schedule artifact that assembled those into local-only focus windows with explicit no-calendar/no-external-write guarantees and a receipt.

Changes:

- Added `data/private-schedule.js` with `local-private-chief-of-staff-schedule`.
- Added `scripts/private-schedule.mjs` and `npm run schedule:private`, which starts a temporary localhost server with `ENABLE_PRIVATE_COCKPIT=1`, reads `/api/private/schedule`, and writes a local receipt to `var/private-schedule-receipts.json`.
- Added gated private route `/api/private/schedule`.
- Added the schedule to `/api/private/cockpit` when the local private gate is enabled.
- Added `/api/private/schedule` to the private route manifest so runtime-surface probes confirm it returns 404 by default.
- Added README docs, package syntax coverage, validator coverage, and API contract coverage.
- The schedule now emits 11 local-only blocks across lane triage, review sessions, and briefing drafts, with:
  - `calendarWrite:false`
  - `externalWrite:false`
  - `manualOnly:true`
  - verification commands
  - exit criteria
  - unscheduled work disclosure
  - a recorder side-effect boundary

## Completed Wave 67

Wave 67: derived Wave 76, opportunity engine de-risking phase 5.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 76 asked to improve opportunity fit, requirements, missing proof, effort, or upside from public-safe evidence. The app already had opportunity packages, quality scoring, and a board, but it did not have one public-safe de-risking surface that converted each package's blockers, requirements, proof bundle, board gate, and manual-use boundary into a prioritized risk-reduction plan with a receipt.

Changes:

- Added `data/opportunity-derisking.js` with `proof-backed-opportunity-derisking`.
- Added public routes:
  - `/api/opportunity-derisking`
  - `/api/opportunity-derisking/:id`
  - `/api/opportunity-derisking/plan`
  - `/api/opportunity-derisking/history`
- Added `scripts/opportunity-derisking.mjs` and `npm run derisk:opportunities`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/opportunity-derisking`, and writes receipts to `var/opportunity-derisking-receipts.json`.
- Added terminal command `opportunity-derisking [id]` with aliases `derisk-opportunities` and `derisk`.
- Added the visible terminal shortcut `opportunity-derisking`.
- Added the de-risking endpoint to the runtime route manifest and safe evidence refresh plan.
- Added README docs, package syntax coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, evidence-refresh summary support, terminal command coverage, runtime-surface target coverage, and Playwright shortcut coverage.
- Every opportunity package now receives a de-risking plan with:
  - risk score and residual risk
  - current gate/readiness/quality/effort/upside context
  - risk factors
  - five de-risking steps
  - forbidden automation rules
  - manual review gate
  - source trace
  - verification command

## Completed Wave 68

Wave 68: derived Wave 77, runtime truth explanation phase 5.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 77 asked for a local or deploy-aware runtime truth check with a receipt, API surface, or terminal explanation. The app already had runtime fingerprints, surface probes, boundary checks, attestation, and reconciliation, but it did not have a public-safe explanation layer that translated those receipts into human-readable proof claims plus explicit non-claims.

Changes:

- Added `data/runtime-explain.js` with `runtime-truth-explanation`.
- Added public routes:
  - `/api/runtime-explain`
  - `/api/runtime-explain/plan`
  - `/api/runtime-explain/history`
- Added `scripts/runtime-explain.mjs` and `npm run explain:runtime`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/runtime-explain`, and writes receipts to `var/runtime-explain-receipts.json`.
- Added terminal command `runtime-explain` with aliases `explain-runtime` and `truth-explain`.
- Added the visible terminal shortcut `runtime-explain`.
- Added the explainer endpoint to the runtime route manifest and safe evidence refresh plan.
- Added README docs, package syntax coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, evidence-refresh summary support, terminal command coverage, runtime-reconciliation truth-route coverage, and Playwright shortcut coverage.
- The explainer now emits:
  - identity and volatile hash explanation
  - receipt-by-receipt proof and limits
  - route and private-boundary explanation
  - five proof claims
  - explicit non-claims for CDN/provider/deploy/private/external limits
  - repair actions and verification commands

## Completed Wave 69

Wave 69: derived Wave 78, design system stabilization phase 5.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 78 asked to improve dense, accessible, mobile-safe, keyboard-first UI without hiding uncertainty. The command center already had a usability-quality evaluator and terminal shortcuts, but it did not have a dedicated design-stability surface that could prove dense control geometry, keyboard affordances, mobile overflow safety, uncertainty disclosure, route coverage, refresh coverage, and receipt recording as one public-safe artifact.

Changes:

- Added `data/design-stability.js` with `command-center-design-stability`.
- Added `scripts/design-stability.mjs` and `npm run audit:design-stability`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/design-stability`, and writes receipts to `var/design-stability-receipts.json`.
- Added public routes:
  - `/api/design-stability`
  - `/api/design-stability/plan`
  - `/api/design-stability/history`
- Added terminal command `design-stability` with aliases `design-audit` and `ui-stability`.
- Added the visible terminal shortcut `design-stability`.
- Hardened `.terminal-shortcuts button` with centered inline-flex layout, fixed minimum height, tighter line-height, zero letter spacing, wrapping, and stable text alignment.
- Added exact CSS source parsing for design-stability signals so combined selector rules do not masquerade as the specific dense shortcut block.
- Added the design endpoint to the runtime route manifest and safe evidence refresh plan.
- Added README docs, package script/check coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, terminal command coverage, Playwright shortcut coverage, and local receipt history.
- The report now emits:
  - dense control ledger
  - keyboard contract
  - mobile contract
  - uncertainty contract
  - explicit non-claims about assistive technology, production browser diversity, proof gaps, and visitor analytics
  - repair actions and verification commands

## Completed Wave 70

Wave 70: derived Wave 79, research-grade evaluation integrity phase 5.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 79 asked for a repeatable research-grade evaluation for truthfulness, usability, performance, accessibility, or proof quality. The app already had many evaluators, but it did not have a meta-evaluation that checked whether those evaluators remained calibrated with each other across proof quality, retrieval, opportunity safety, usability/design, accessibility/performance receipts, runtime reconciliation, and research stress.

Changes:

- Added `data/evaluation-integrity.js` with `research-grade-evaluation-integrity`.
- Added `scripts/evaluation-integrity.mjs` and `npm run audit:evaluation-integrity`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/evaluation/integrity`, and writes receipts to `var/evaluation-integrity-receipts.json`.
- Added public routes:
  - `/api/evaluation/integrity`
  - `/api/evaluation/integrity/plan`
  - `/api/evaluation/integrity/history`
- Added terminal command `evaluation-integrity` with aliases `eval-integrity` and `research-grade`.
- Added the visible terminal shortcut `evaluation-integrity`.
- Added the integrity endpoint to the runtime route manifest and safe evidence refresh plan.
- Added README docs, package script/check coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, evidence-refresh summary support, terminal command coverage, and Playwright shortcut coverage.
- The report now emits:
  - six evaluation domains: truthfulness, retrieval, opportunity, usability-design, accessibility-performance, and runtime-stress
  - eight integrity checks
  - a calibration matrix
  - a repeatability contract with explicit forbidden actions
  - explicit non-claims about human review, screen-reader certification, production RUM/CDN behavior, and private data
  - repair actions and verification commands
