## Completed Wave 71

Wave 71: derived Wave 80, narrative intelligence disclosure phase 5.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 80 asked to generate or validate narrative output from evidence with uncertainty and repair guidance. The app already had grounded narratives, objection pressure tests, and tailored variants, but it did not have one disclosure layer that validated narrative outputs across claims, artifacts, caveats, objections, tailored variants, manual-use boundaries, prohibited overclaims, and repair guidance.

Changes:

- Added `data/narrative-disclosure.js` with `evidence-narrative-disclosure`.
- Added `scripts/narrative-disclosure.mjs` and `npm run disclose:narratives`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/narrative-disclosure`, and writes receipts to `var/narrative-disclosure-receipts.json`.
- Added public routes:
  - `/api/narrative-disclosure`
  - `/api/narrative-disclosure/:audience`
  - `/api/narrative-disclosure/plan`
  - `/api/narrative-disclosure/history`
- Added terminal command `narrative-disclosure [audience]` with aliases `disclose-narratives` and `disclosures`.
- Added the visible terminal shortcut `narrative-disclosure`.
- Added the disclosure endpoint to the runtime route manifest and safe evidence refresh plan.
- Added README docs, package script/check coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, evidence-refresh summary support, terminal command coverage, and Playwright shortcut coverage.
- The report now emits:
  - audience-specific disclosure bundles for recruiter, professor, and founder
  - evidence grounding with claim IDs, artifact IDs, source trails, and caveats
  - objection coverage and tailored output coverage
  - must-disclose lists
  - repair guidance
  - prohibited overclaims
  - a disclosure queue
  - explicit non-claims that prevent automatic sending, publishing, external outcome inference, or private-reference escalation

## Completed Wave 72

Wave 72: derived Wave 81, evidence graph normalization depth phase 6.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 81 asked for one verifiable relationship, confidence rule, or projection guard in the evidence graph. The app had narrative disclosure bundles, but those disclosures were not yet exposed as their own graph-depth relationship surface with confidence caps, route coverage, refresh coverage, terminal access, and receipts.

Changes:

- Added `data/graph-disclosure-links.js` with `evidence-graph-disclosure-links`.
- Added `scripts/graph-disclosure-links.mjs` and `npm run audit:graph-disclosures`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/graph-disclosure-links`, and writes receipts to `var/graph-disclosure-links-receipts.json`.

  - `/api/graph-disclosure-links`
  - `/api/graph-disclosure-links/plan`

- Added terminal command `graph-disclosures` with aliases `graph-disclosure` and `disclosure-links`.

- Added the graph disclosure endpoint to the runtime route manifest and safe evidence refresh plan.
- Added README docs, package script/check coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, evidence-refresh summary support, terminal command coverage, and Playwright shortcut coverage.
- The report now projects narrative disclosures into public-safe graph relationships:
  - `disclosure-validates-narrative`
  - `disclosure-cites-claim`
  - `disclosure-cites-artifact`
  - `disclosure-answers-objection`
  - `disclosure-has-repair`
- Added a confidence rule: every disclosure graph relationship caps confidence at the lowest available score from the disclosure bundle and its backing narrative, claim, artifact, objection, or repair guidance.

## Completed Wave 73

Wave 73: derived Wave 82, artifact museum capture phase 6.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 82 asked to add or validate one public-safe artifact type, screenshot, transcript, replay, or gap record. The museum already had generated previews, API replays, terminal replays, transcripts, links, and screenshot gap records; it did not yet have a first-class per-project museum capture artifact proving that each project has an inspectable public-safe museum readiness path before richer media is claimed.

Changes:

- Added a new public-safe artifact type, `museum-capture`, to `data/artifact-model.js`.
- Each project now gets a `${project}.museum-capture` artifact with:
  - `mediaKind: audit-record`
  - `url: /api/artifact-museum`
  - `command: artifact-museum`
  - a capture policy explicitly saying it is not a screenshot, video, private file, or raw production trace
  - source traces to the project record, artifact museum API route, and terminal command
- Added `counts.museumCaptures` to the artifact catalog.
- Added a new `museum-captures` artifact collection with a curator note and suggested inspection path.
- Added `museumCaptures` to the artifact museum summary.
- Added a `capture-readiness` museum dimension.
- Added a `museum-capture-coverage` museum check requiring every project to expose a public-safe capture artifact.
- Updated the `artifacts` terminal command to report museum capture counts.
- Added validator and API contract coverage for the new artifact type, collection, and museum check.

Evidence:

- `npm run check` passed after implementation: `Validated 13 projects, 74 claims, 77 artifacts, 13 checks, 41 private references.`
- `node --test test/api-contract.test.mjs` passed after Wave 73: 9 tests, 1 suite, 0 failures.

## Completed Wave 74

Wave 74: derived Wave 83, private chief-of-staff prioritization phase 6.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 83 asked to improve local/private planning without exposing private documents or sending anything automatically. The app already had next actions, tasks, review sessions, briefing drafts, and a local schedule, but it did not have a single private chief-of-staff priority stack that merged those planning surfaces into ranked now/next/later decisions with lane coverage, manual-only guardrails, forbidden actions, and its own receipt.

Changes:

- Added `data/private-priorities.js` with `local-private-chief-of-staff-prioritization`.
- Added `scripts/private-priorities.mjs` and `npm run prioritize:private`, which starts a temporary localhost server with `ENABLE_PRIVATE_COCKPIT=1`, reads `/api/private/priorities`, and writes receipts to `var/private-priority-receipts.json`.
- Added gated local/private route `/api/private/priorities`.
- Added the priorities report to the private cockpit payload.
- Added `/api/private/priorities` to the private route manifest, which expands public-disabled private gate coverage.
- Added README docs, package script/check coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, runtime surface private-gate coverage, and receipt coverage.
- The prioritization report now:
  - merges next actions, private tasks, schedule blocks, review sessions, briefing drafts, privacy approvals, outreach drafts, and chief-of-staff lanes
  - ranks a 24-item priority stack
  - preserves coverage across proof repair, privacy approval, review sessions, briefing drafts, and outreach review lanes
  - groups work into `now`, `next`, and `later` decision lanes
  - creates a `todayFocus` from the top priority and its aligned schedule block
  - enforces manual-only, review-required, external-write-disabled priority items
  - attaches forbidden actions including sending, scheduling, submitting, approving publication, deploying production, and spending money
  - records explicit non-external side-effect boundaries in its plan and receipt

Evidence:

- `npm run check` passed after implementation: `Validated 13 projects, 74 claims, 77 artifacts, 13 checks, 41 private references.`
- First `npm run prioritize:private` produced `private-priority-mpoa0kxt 8/8 check(s) passed; 24 priority item(s); score 100/100; wrote var/private-priority-receipts.json`.

## Completed Wave 75

Wave 75: derived Wave 84, opportunity ranking phase 6.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 84 asked to improve opportunity fit, requirements, missing proof, effort, or upside from public-safe evidence. The app already had an archetype radar, packages, a proof-backed board, de-risking plans, and quality scoring, but it did not have a single public-safe decision engine that fused those signals into a ranked next-best local proof-repair queue with its own route, command, receipt, validator, tests, and refresh coverage.

Changes:

- Added `data/opportunity-ranking.js` with `proof-backed-opportunity-ranking`.
- Added `scripts/opportunity-ranking.mjs` and `npm run rank:opportunities`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/opportunity-ranking`, and writes receipts to `var/opportunity-ranking-receipts.json`.
- Added public API routes:
  - `/api/opportunity-ranking`
  - `/api/opportunity-ranking/:id`
  - `/api/opportunity-ranking/plan`
  - `/api/opportunity-ranking/history`
- Added terminal command `opportunity-ranking [id]` with aliases `opportunity-rank` and `rank-opportunities`.
- Added the visible terminal shortcut `opportunity-ranking`.
- Added `/api/opportunity-ranking` to the safe evidence refresh plan and all ranking routes to the runtime route manifest.
- Added a runtime-surface concrete sample for `/api/opportunity-ranking/:id` so route probes use `agent-infra-internship` instead of the generic `recruiter-vs-professor` dynamic ID sample.
- Added README docs, package script/check coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, E2E shortcut coverage, runtime-surface coverage, and receipt coverage.
- The ranking report now:
  - ranks all six opportunity packages by fit, readiness, quality, proof depth, effort, upside, residual risk, board gate, blockers, and missing requirements
  - exposes inspectable `rankFactors` and `whyRanked` text for each package
  - groups ranked packages into manual-only decision lanes: `repair-now`, `repair-next`, and `hold-until-proof`
  - creates a `missingProofQueue` with local-only/read-only actions and verification commands
  - creates a requirement gap matrix for every ranked package
  - carries explicit manual-only boundaries, false live-posting/application-state flags, and forbidden actions including send, submit, schedule, recipient-interest claims, and third-party writes

Evidence:

- `npm run check` passed after implementation: `Validated 13 projects, 74 claims, 77 artifacts, 13 checks, 41 private references.`
- First `npm run rank:opportunities` produced `opportunity-ranking-mponnnv1 10/10 check(s) passed; 6 ranking(s); top agent-infra-internship; score 100/100; wrote var/opportunity-ranking-receipts.json`.
- `node --test test/api-contract.test.mjs` passed after Wave 75: 9 tests, 1 suite, 0 failures.
- Initial `npm run record:runtime-surface` correctly failed with `surface-mponofb1 126/127 passed` because `/api/opportunity-ranking/:id` probed `recruiter-vs-professor`; after adding the concrete route sample, `npm run record:runtime-surface` produced `surface-mponp2hv 127/127 passed; 11/11 private gate probe(s); wrote var/runtime-surface-receipts.json`.

## Completed Wave 76

Wave 76: derived Wave 85, runtime deploy-readiness truth phase 6.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 85 asked for a local or deploy-aware runtime truth check with a receipt, API surface, or terminal explanation. The app already had runtime fingerprints, route-surface probes, public/private boundary checks, reconciliation, and runtime explanations, but it did not have a single deploy-readiness packet that says: "the local runtime is ready to be manually compared against a hosted runtime" without pretending that a deploy provider, CDN, or production dashboard was inspected.

Changes:

- Added `data/runtime-deploy-readiness.js` with `runtime-deploy-readiness`.
- Added `scripts/runtime-deploy-readiness.mjs` and `npm run audit:runtime-deploy`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/runtime-deploy-readiness`, and writes receipts to `var/runtime-deploy-readiness-receipts.json`.
- Added public API routes:
  - `/api/runtime-deploy-readiness`
  - `/api/runtime-deploy-readiness/plan`
  - `/api/runtime-deploy-readiness/history`
- Added terminal command `runtime-deploy` with aliases `runtime-deploy-readiness` and `deploy-readiness`.
- Added the visible terminal shortcut `runtime-deploy`.
- Added `/api/runtime-deploy-readiness` to the safe evidence refresh plan, runtime route manifest, and runtime reconciliation truth-route ledger.
- Added README docs, package script/check coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, E2E shortcut coverage, runtime-surface coverage, and receipt coverage.
- The deploy-readiness report now:
  - builds six gates for runtime identity, route surface, evidence refresh, private boundary, attestation, and verification scripts
  - creates a `comparisonPacket` with local identity hash, package/build identity, route counts, refresh counts, and latest runtime/surface/refresh receipts
  - exposes a before/after deploy handoff checklist that stays local-only or human read-only
  - states non-claims for no deploy, no provider/CDN proof, no private cockpit reads, and no production parity without human comparison
  - preserves explicit side-effect boundaries: no deploy, no publish, no git mutation, no provider dashboard query, no CDN inspection, no private gate enabling, and no third-party contact

Evidence:

- `npm run check` passed after implementation: `Validated 13 projects, 74 claims, 77 artifacts, 13 checks, 41 private references.`
- First `npm run audit:runtime-deploy` produced `runtime-deploy-readiness-mponzn78 6/7 check(s) passed; 5/6 gate(s); score 83/100` because the runtime identity receipt was stale after adding new runtime files. ## Completed Wave 77

Wave 77: derived Wave 86, keyboard-first dense UI phase 6.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 86 asked to improve dense, accessible, mobile-safe, keyboard-first UI without hiding uncertainty. The app already had dense terminal shortcuts, a design-stability evaluator, and uncertainty surfaces, but it did not have direct global keyboard access to the command center or a dedicated readiness receipt that proves skip targets, keyboard shortcuts, mobile command geometry, semantic terminal controls, public routes, refresh coverage, and visible uncertainty as one public-safe artifact.

Changes:

- Added a focus-only skip-link cluster in `index.html` for search, terminal, and selected works.
- Added the visible terminal shortcut `keyboard-readiness`.
- Added source-level terminal shortcut labeling in `command-center.mjs`, giving each command button a generated `title` and `aria-label`.
- Added global keyboard handling in `command-center.mjs`:
  - `/` focuses and selects the command search when not already typing in an editable control.
  - Backquote focuses and selects the terminal command input when not already typing in an editable control.
  - `Escape` blurs the current editable control.
  - Modifier chords and editable fields are ignored so regular typing is preserved.
- Added mobile command-center CSS hardening:
  - skip links now reveal with `:focus-visible`
  - mobile terminal shortcuts use a smaller responsive minimum track
  - existing single-column mobile command forms and overflow wrapping remain guarded
- Added `data/keyboard-readiness.js` with `command-center-keyboard-readiness`.
- Added `scripts/keyboard-readiness.mjs` and `npm run audit:keyboard-readiness`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/keyboard-readiness`, and writes receipts to `var/keyboard-readiness-receipts.json`.
- Added public API routes:
  - `/api/keyboard-readiness`
  - `/api/keyboard-readiness/plan`
  - `/api/keyboard-readiness/history`
- Added terminal command `keyboard-readiness` with aliases `keyboard` and `command-keyboard`.
- Added `/api/keyboard-readiness` to the safe evidence refresh plan and all keyboard-readiness routes to the runtime route manifest.
- Added README docs, package script/check coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, E2E keyboard coverage, runtime-surface coverage, and receipt coverage.
- The keyboard-readiness report now:
  - checks skip links to command search, terminal, and works
  - checks global slash/backquote/Escape shortcuts and editable-field safety
  - checks native terminal button semantics and generated labels
  - checks mobile command geometry and existing overflow guards
  - checks proof ribbon/truth ledger/missing-proof visibility so keyboard polish does not hide uncertainty
  - carries explicit non-claims for screen-reader parity, production/CDN behavior, weak-proof concealment, visitor analytics, keystroke collection, and private cockpit data

Evidence:

- `npm run check` passed after implementation: `Validated 13 projects, 74 claims, 77 artifacts, 13 checks, 41 private references.`

After fixing the source signal, `npm run audit:keyboard-readiness` produced `keyboard-readiness-mpoob45j 9/9 check(s) passed; 3 shortcut(s); score 100/100; wrote var/keyboard-readiness-receipts.json`.
- `node --test test/api-contract.test.mjs` passed after Wave 77: 9 tests, 1 suite, 0 failures.
- `npm run test:e2e` passed after Wave 77: 2 Playwright tests, including the new skip-link focus, slash search focus, backquote terminal focus, visible keyboard-readiness shortcut, generated `aria-label`, and mobile no-horizontal-overflow checks.

## Completed Wave 78

Wave 78: derived Wave 87, claim-calibration benchmark phase 6.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 87 asked for a repeatable evaluation for truthfulness, usability, performance, accessibility, or proof quality. The app already had aggregate proof quality and search/evaluation integrity scores, but it did not have a claim-level benchmark that verifies every public claim is calibrated to its modeled evidence strength, source material, freshness, private-reference boundary, wording risk, and repair guidance.

Changes:

- Added `data/claim-calibration.js` with `claim-calibration-benchmark`.
- Added `scripts/claim-calibration.mjs` and `npm run audit:claim-calibration`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/evaluation/claim-calibration`, and writes receipts to `var/claim-calibration-receipts.json`.
- Added public API routes:
  - `/api/evaluation/claim-calibration`
  - `/api/evaluation/claim-calibration/plan`
  - `/api/evaluation/claim-calibration/history`
- Added terminal command `claim-calibration` with aliases `claims-calibration` and `claim-benchmark`.
- Added the visible terminal shortcut `claim-calibration`.
- Added `/api/evaluation/claim-calibration` to the safe evidence refresh plan and all claim-calibration routes to the runtime route manifest.
- Added README docs, package script/check coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, E2E shortcut coverage, runtime-surface coverage, and receipt coverage.
- The claim-calibration report now:
  - calibrates all 74 public claim projections
  - assigns each claim a `publish-ready`, `caveat-required`, or `repair-required` band
  - ensures needs-source claims are never promoted beyond repair-required
  - keeps private-reference claims public-safe
  - checks source material, repair guidance, route coverage, refresh coverage, script coverage, and proof-quality coherence
  - exposes a repair queue for non-publish-ready claims
  - states non-claims for no external fact-checking, no private-file inspection, no manual-review replacement, and no promotion of weak/private claims

Evidence:

- `npm run check` passed after implementation: `Validated 13 projects, 74 claims, 77 artifacts, 13 checks, 41 private references.`

## Completed Wave 79

Wave 79: derived Wave 88, narrative grounding receipt phase 6.

Rationale:

The app already generated grounded narratives and had downstream tailoring/disclosure receipts, but the grounding layer itself had no repeatable plan/history/script/receipt surface proving that every audience narrative remained evidence-linked, caveated, repair-guided, draft-only, route-covered, and refresh-covered.

## Completed Wave 80

Wave 80: derived Wave 89, evidence graph confidence guard phase 7.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 89 asked for one verifiable relationship, confidence rule, or projection guard in the evidence graph. The app already had graph crosslinks, disclosure graph links, and tailored narrative projection guards, but there was no receipt-backed surface that audited all three families together for confidence bounds, high-confidence explicitness, inferred-project caps, disclosure confidence caps, public-safe projection, route coverage, refresh coverage, and script coverage.

Changes:

- Added `data/graph-confidence.js` with `evidence-graph-confidence-guard`.
- Added `scripts/graph-confidence.mjs` and `npm run audit:graph-confidence`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/graph-confidence`, and writes receipts to `var/graph-confidence-receipts.json`.
- Added public API routes:
  - `/api/graph-confidence`
  - `/api/graph-confidence/plan`
  - `/api/graph-confidence/history`
- Added terminal command `graph-confidence` with aliases `confidence-guard` and `graph-confidence-guard`.
- Added the visible terminal shortcut `graph-confidence`.
- Added `/api/graph-confidence` to the safe evidence refresh plan and all graph-confidence routes to the runtime route manifest.
- Added README docs, package script/check coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, E2E shortcut coverage, runtime-surface coverage, and receipt coverage.
- The graph confidence guard now combines crosslink, disclosure, and projection relationships into a single public-safe report with:
  - explicit-ID high-confidence rule
  - inferred project cap at `<= 88`
  - disclosure confidence cap at the lowest backing score
  - projection guard coherence check for source resolution, quarantine, and low-confidence draft boundaries
  - non-claims for no production graph persistence proof, no private inspection, and no audience/outcome overclaims

- `npm run check` passed after implementation: `Validated 13 projects, 74 claims, 77 artifacts, 13 checks, 41 private references.`

## Completed Wave 82

Wave 82: derived Wave 91, private chief-of-staff readiness receipt phase 7.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 91 asked to improve local/private chief-of-staff planning without exposing private documents or sending anything automatically. The app already had local private next actions, tasks, review sessions, briefing drafts, schedule receipts, and priority receipts, but the top-level chief-of-staff readiness report itself did not have a gated plan/history/script/receipt surface proving the whole planning system remained local-only, review-required, and external-write-disabled.

Changes:

- Extended `data/private-chief-of-staff.js` with:
  - `privateChiefOfStaffPlan()`
  - local receipt read/write helpers
  - report-level plan and verification command metadata
  - summary fields for private route coverage and latest receipt id
  - checks for chief-of-staff private plan/history route coverage and `chief:private` package script coverage
- Added `scripts/private-chief-of-staff.mjs` and `npm run chief:private`, which starts a temporary localhost server with `ENABLE_PRIVATE_COCKPIT=1`, reads `/api/private/chief-of-staff`, and writes receipts to `var/private-chief-of-staff-receipts.json`.
- Added gated local/private routes:
  - `/api/private/chief-of-staff/plan`
  - `/api/private/chief-of-staff/history`
- Kept existing gated local/private route:
  - `/api/private/chief-of-staff`
- Added chief-of-staff plan/history routes to the private route manifest so runtime-surface verifies they return `404` by default on the public path.
- Added README docs, package script/check coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, private-gate coverage, runtime-surface coverage, and receipt coverage.
- Preserved the operating policy that the chief-of-staff surface cannot send messages, schedule events, submit applications, approve publication, deploy production, purchase anything, or mutate third-party systems.

## Completed Wave 83

Wave 83: derived Wave 92, opportunity engine source-quality receipt phase 7.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 92 asked to improve opportunity fit, requirements, missing proof, effort, or upside from public-safe evidence. The app already had opportunity de-risking and ranking receipts, but the upstream opportunity quality evaluator that scores fit explainability, requirement coverage, missing-proof actionability, evidence depth, effort/upside calibration, and manual-only safety did not have its own plan/history/script/receipt surface.

Changes:

- Extended `data/opportunity-quality.js` with:
  - `opportunityQualityPlan()`
  - local receipt read/write helpers
  - report-level plan and verification command metadata
  - summary fields for checks, route coverage, refresh coverage, and latest receipt id
  - checks for opportunity source coverage, package benchmark coverage, requirement status/repair coverage, missing-proof actionability, effort/upside labels, manual-safety boundary, route manifest coverage, refresh plan coverage, and `audit:opportunity-quality` script coverage
- Added `scripts/opportunity-quality.mjs` and `npm run audit:opportunity-quality`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/evaluation/opportunity-quality`, and writes receipts to `var/opportunity-quality-receipts.json`.
- Added public API routes:
  - `/api/evaluation/opportunity-quality/plan`
  - `/api/evaluation/opportunity-quality/history`
- Kept existing public route:
  - `/api/evaluation/opportunity-quality`
- Added opportunity-quality plan/history routes to the runtime route manifest.
- Added README docs, package script/check coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, runtime-surface coverage, and receipt coverage.
- Preserved the source boundary that the opportunity engine is archetype-based and does not claim live postings, deadlines, applications, interviews, funding, recipient interest, outreach status, or external availability.

Evidence:

- `npm run check` passed after implementation: `Validated 13 projects, 74 claims, 77 artifacts, 13 checks, 41 private references.`
- `node --test test/api-contract.test.mjs` passed after Wave 83: 9 tests, 1 suite, 0 failures.
- First `npm run audit:opportunity-quality` produced `opportunity-quality-mpoppspc 9/9 check(s) passed; 6 package(s); score 76/100; wrote var/opportunity-quality-receipts.json`.
- After route expansion and downstream refresh:

  - `npm run refresh:evidence` produced `refresh-1779925554369 35/35 passed; wrote var/evidence-refresh-receipts.json`
  - `npm run derisk:opportunities` produced `opportunity-derisking-mpoppssr 7/7 check(s) passed; 6 plan(s); 27 high-priority step(s); score 100/100; wrote var/opportunity-derisking-receipts.json`
  - `npm run rank:opportunities` produced `opportunity-ranking-mpoppstc 10/10 check(s) passed; 6 ranking(s); top agent-infra-internship; score 100/100; wrote var/opportunity-ranking-receipts.json`
  - Final `npm run audit:opportunity-quality` produced `opportunity-quality-mpopq36g 9/9 check(s) passed; 6 package(s); score 76/100; wrote var/opportunity-quality-receipts.json`
  - `npm run record:runtime` produced `runtime-mpopq363 readiness 91/100; 3 runtime change(s); wrote var/runtime-truth-receipts.json`

## Completed Wave 84

Wave 84: derived Wave 93, runtime evidence chain custody audit.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 93 asked for a local or deploy-aware runtime truth check. The app already had separate runtime truth, surface, boundary, reconciliation, explanation, and deploy-readiness reports. What was missing was a single chain-of-custody layer that proves those pieces are mutually fresh, command-backed, route-covered, refresh-covered, and explicit about what local evidence cannot know.

Changes:

- Added `data/runtime-evidence-chain.js` with:
  - `runtimeEvidenceChainPlan()`
  - local receipt read/write helpers
  - a seven-link chain over runtime fingerprint, runtime attestation, route surface, public/private boundary, receipt reconciliation, human explanation, and deploy-readiness
  - custody packet fields for identity hash, volatile hash, runtime/surface/refresh/explain/deploy receipt ids, and manual comparison rules
  - checks for runtime fingerprint freshness, attestation/boundary confidence, surface receipt currency, reconciliation freshness, explanation non-claims, deploy handoff readiness, chain link depth, custody receipt ids, route coverage, refresh coverage, script coverage, and overclaim boundaries
- Added `scripts/runtime-evidence-chain.mjs` and `npm run audit:runtime-chain`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/runtime-evidence-chain`, and writes receipts to `var/runtime-evidence-chain-receipts.json`.
- Added public API routes:
  - `/api/runtime-evidence-chain`
  - `/api/runtime-evidence-chain/plan`
  - `/api/runtime-evidence-chain/history`
- Added `runtime-chain` terminal command aliases.
- Added runtime evidence chain routes to the runtime route manifest.
- Added `/api/runtime-evidence-chain` to the safe evidence refresh plan.
- Added README docs, package script/check coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, runtime-surface coverage, refresh coverage, terminal coverage, and receipt coverage.
- Preserved the source boundary that this proves local receipt coherence only. It does not deploy, publish, mutate git history, inspect private cockpit data, query provider dashboards, inspect CDN state, prove DNS propagation, prove external uptime, or claim hosted parity without manual comparison.

Evidence:

- `npm run check` passed after implementation: `Validated 13 projects, 74 claims, 77 artifacts, 13 checks, 41 private references.`
- `node --test test/api-contract.test.mjs` passed after Wave 84: 9 tests, 1 suite, 0 failures.
- Receipt refresh sequence:

  - `npm run record:runtime-surface` produced `surface-mpoq0kph 150/150 passed; 13/13 private gate probe(s); wrote var/runtime-surface-receipts.json`
  - `npm run refresh:evidence` produced `refresh-1779926060244 36/36 passed; wrote var/evidence-refresh-receipts.json`
  - `npm run explain:runtime` produced `runtime-explain-mpoq0vg2 6/6 check(s) passed; 5 proof claim(s); score 100/100; wrote var/runtime-explain-receipts.json`
  - `npm run audit:runtime-deploy` produced `runtime-deploy-readiness-mpoq0xva 7/7 check(s) passed; 6/6 gate(s); score 100/100; wrote var/runtime-deploy-readiness-receipts.json`
  - First `npm run audit:runtime-chain` produced `runtime-chain-mpoq123e 12/12 check(s) passed; 7 link(s); score 100/100; wrote var/runtime-evidence-chain-receipts.json`
- `npm run verify` passed after Wave 84: check, inline build with `536792 bytes`, 9 API tests, and 2 Playwright E2E tests.

## Completed Wave 85

Wave 85: derived Wave 94, design ambition compression phase 7.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 94 asked to continue the Design ambition pillar. The app already had design stability and keyboard readiness audits, but it did not have a single design-system ambition layer that verifies the command center compresses proof orientation, runtime truth, keyboard density, responsive resilience, and uncertainty preservation into a reachable first-screen/control surface.

Changes:

- Added `data/design-ambition.js` with:
  - `designAmbitionPlan()`
  - local receipt read/write helpers
  - a five-family design ambition contract over proof orientation, runtime truth access, keyboard density, mobile control resilience, and uncertainty preservation
  - checks for design-system scores, visible runtime-chain access, critical shortcut compression, first-screen proof compression, responsive density, control-family depth, route manifest coverage, refresh coverage, script coverage, and non-claim boundaries
- Added `scripts/design-ambition.mjs` and `npm run audit:design-ambition`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/design-ambition`, and writes receipts to `var/design-ambition-receipts.json`.

  - `ambition-design`
  - `command-design`
- Added visible terminal shortcuts:
  - `runtime-chain`
  - `design-ambition`
- Added design ambition routes to the runtime route manifest.
- Added `/api/design-ambition` to the safe evidence refresh plan.
- Added README docs, package script/check coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, E2E shortcut coverage, runtime-surface coverage, refresh coverage, terminal coverage, and receipt coverage.
- The first design ambition receipt correctly failed at `7/10` checks because the report was using the narrower design source signal set and could not see truth-ledger/mobile keyboard signals. Fixed the source wiring to use the richer keyboard/design signal bundle, then reran to `10/10`.
- Preserved the source boundary that design ambition does not claim live visitor comprehension, production analytics, full assistive-technology parity, CDN behavior, cross-browser device-lab coverage, admissions/hiring/funding/interview outcomes, or private data access.

## Completed Wave 86

Wave 86: derived Wave 95, research-grade evaluation rigor phase 7.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 95 asked to continue the Research-grade evaluation pillar. The app already had proof quality, search quality, opportunity quality, usability quality, research stress, evaluation integrity, runtime evidence chain, and design ambition audits, but it did not have one public-safe meta-rigor layer that treats those evaluators as falsifiable research instruments with methodology, source traceability, adversarial pressure, runtime/design coherence, and explicit non-claims.

Changes:

- Added `data/research-rigor.js` with:
  - `researchRigorPlan()`
  - local receipt read/write helpers
  - a five-dimension research rigor contract over methodology disclosure, source traceability, adversarial stress, runtime/design coherence, and public-safe limitations
  - checks for dimension pass rate, methodology repeatability, current stress/integrity receipts, source-traced benchmarks, manual opportunity boundaries, runtime/design coherence, non-claim boundaries, route manifest coverage, refresh coverage, and script coverage
- Added `scripts/research-rigor.mjs` and `npm run audit:research-rigor`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/evaluation/research-rigor`, and writes receipts to `var/research-rigor-receipts.json`.
- Added public API routes:
  - `/api/evaluation/research-rigor`
  - `/api/evaluation/research-rigor/plan`
  - `/api/evaluation/research-rigor/history`
- Added terminal command aliases:
  - `research-rigor`
  - `rigor`
  - `evaluation-rigor`
- Added a visible `research-rigor` terminal shortcut.
- Added research rigor routes to the runtime route manifest.
- Added `/api/evaluation/research-rigor` to the safe evidence refresh plan.
- Added README docs, package script/check coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, E2E shortcut coverage, runtime-surface coverage, refresh coverage, terminal coverage, and receipt coverage.
- The first live `npm run audit:research-rigor` correctly failed at `7/10` checks and `3/5` dimensions because dependent runtime/design/stress/integrity receipts were stale after the new route and shortcut. Refreshed the full dependency chain, then reran to `10/10`.
- Preserved the source boundary that research rigor does not claim live user research, independent peer review, admissions/hiring/funding review, production RUM, CDN/provider/DNS/external uptime, accessibility certification, private cockpit access, private files, credentials, inboxes, calendars, or third-party account state.

## Completed Wave 87

Wave 87: derived Wave 96, narrative intelligence sequencing phase 7.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 96 asked to continue the Narrative intelligence pillar. The app already had grounded narratives, contrast guidance, objection pressure tests, tailored variants, and disclosure bundles. What was missing was a conductor layer that proves each audience narrative is said in a safe order: thesis, proof, contrast, objection pressure, disclosure, tailored draft, and manual next step.

Changes:

- Added `data/narrative-sequence.js` with:
  - `narrativeSequencePlan()`
  - local receipt read/write helpers
  - selector aliases for recruiter, professor/research, and founder/startup audiences
  - a sequence contract requiring disclosure and manual-use boundaries before action-oriented drafts
  - seven ordered beats per audience: lead thesis, proof path, audience contrast, objection pressure, disclosure gate, tailored draft, and manual next step
  - per-sequence checks for beat depth, evidence grounding, proof-before-action order, disclosure-before-action order, objection-before-tailor order, claim/artifact coverage, disclosure depth, and manual-only tailor depth
  - report checks for audience coverage, sequence depth, beat grounding, disclosure ordering, objection/tailor coherence, route manifest coverage, refresh coverage, and script coverage
- Added `scripts/narrative-sequence.mjs` and `npm run sequence:narratives`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/narrative-sequence`, and writes receipts to `var/narrative-sequence-receipts.json`.
- Added public API routes:

  - `/api/narrative-sequence/history`
- Added terminal command aliases:
  - `narrative-sequence`

- Added a visible `narrative-sequence` terminal shortcut.
- Added narrative sequence routes to the runtime route manifest.
- Added `/api/narrative-sequence` to the safe evidence refresh plan.
- Added README docs, package script/check coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, E2E shortcut coverage, runtime-surface coverage, refresh coverage, terminal coverage, and receipt coverage.
- Preserved the source boundary that narrative sequencing does not approve automatic sending, publishing, applications, submissions, outreach, private artifact approvals, analytics collection, recipient interest, interviews, admissions, funding, hiring outcomes, application outcomes, or private cockpit access.

## Completed Wave 88

Wave 88: derived Wave 97, evidence graph depth scoring phase 8.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 97 asked to continue the Evidence graph depth pillar. The app already had graph quality, crosslinks, normalization scoreboard, lineage, projection guard, disclosure links, and confidence guard. What was missing was a depth score that unifies those layers and answers: how far can public-safe evidence travel from graph structure into proof, narrative objections, disclosures, confidence caps, repair actions, and manual sequence gates?

Changes:

- Added `data/graph-depth-score.js` with:
  - `graphDepthScorePlan()`
  - local receipt read/write helpers
  - a graph depth policy for deep path shape, scoring, and safety boundaries
  - six depth lanes: structural graph spine, normalization and repair horizon, narrative objection lineage depth, disclosure relationship depth, confidence policy depth, and narrative sequence bridge
  - a depth map over identity-to-proof, narrative-to-objection, disclosure-to-repair, and confidence-to-sequence paths
  - checks for lane coverage, structural quality, lineage depth, disclosure depth, confidence depth, sequence bridge coverage, low-normalization repair visibility, route manifest coverage, refresh coverage, and script coverage
- Added `scripts/graph-depth-score.mjs` and `npm run audit:graph-depth`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/graph-depth-score`, and writes receipts to `var/graph-depth-score-receipts.json`.
- Added public API routes:
  - `/api/graph-depth-score`
  - `/api/graph-depth-score/plan`
  - `/api/graph-depth-score/history`
- Added terminal command aliases:
  - `graph-depth`
  - `graph-depth-score`
  - `evidence-depth`
- Added a visible `graph-depth` terminal shortcut.
- Added graph depth routes to the runtime route manifest.
- Added `/api/graph-depth-score` to the safe evidence refresh plan.
- Added README docs, package script/check coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, E2E shortcut coverage, runtime-surface coverage, refresh coverage, terminal coverage, and receipt coverage.
- Preserved the source boundary that graph depth does not prove production graph persistence, hosted graph rendering, deployed graph storage, private graph storage, private files, credentials, dashboards, inboxes, unapproved artifacts, audience interest, admissions, hiring, funding, interviews, third-party validation, or complete real-world relationship coverage.
- Kept the low graph normalization score visible as a repair lane instead of hiding it. The depth score passes because structural quality, lineage, disclosure relationships, confidence caps, and narrative sequence bridges are strong while graph-scoreboard still exposes repair actions for broader rendered coverage.

## Completed Wave 89

Wave 89: derived Wave 98, artifact museum comparison phase 8.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 98 asked to compare artifact museum depth. The app already had a public-safe artifact catalog, curated collections, transcripts, replay walks, project-to-project artifact comparison, and a curator-level museum audit. What was missing was a comparison audit for the museum itself: collection lane versus collection lane, replay continuity versus transcript continuity, explicit media gap honesty versus proof curation, and repeatable route/script/receipt coverage for that comparison surface.

Changes:

- Added `data/artifact-museum-compare.js` with:
  - `artifactMuseumComparisonPlan()`
  - local receipt read/write helpers
  - collection-lane scoring across projects, artifacts, gaps, transcript backing, replay backing, source traces, proof strength, and approval-required artifacts
  - a 21-pair comparison matrix over museum collections
  - focus-pair prioritization for high-signal curator decisions
  - dimensions for matrix depth, collection lane coverage, project coverage, replay continuity, transcript continuity, gap honesty, public safety, and repeatable verification
  - checks for lane coverage, comparison depth, project coverage, replay/transcript continuity, gap honesty, public-safe source traces, route manifest coverage, refresh coverage, receipt route coverage, and script coverage
  - `selectArtifactMuseumComparisonPair()` for pair lookup
- Added `scripts/artifact-museum-compare.mjs` and `npm run compare:artifact-museum`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/artifact-museum-compare`, and writes receipts to `var/artifact-museum-compare-receipts.json`.
- Added public API routes:
  - `/api/artifact-museum-compare`
  - `/api/artifact-museum-compare/plan`
  - `/api/artifact-museum-compare/history`
  - `/api/artifact-museum-compare?left=proof-strongest&right=media-replay`
- Added terminal command aliases:
  - `artifact-museum-compare`
  - `museum-compare`
  - `artifact-compare-museum`
- Added a visible `artifact-museum-compare` terminal shortcut.
- Added artifact museum comparison routes to the runtime route manifest.
- Added `/api/artifact-museum-compare` to the safe evidence refresh plan.
- Added README docs, package script/check coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, E2E shortcut coverage, runtime-surface coverage, refresh coverage, terminal coverage, and receipt coverage.
- Preserved the source boundary that museum comparison reads only public-safe artifact collections, transcripts, replay records, museum readiness, source traces, and explicit gap records; it does not infer screenshots, videos, private files, external uptime, unpublished media, or unapproved raw evidence.

## Completed Wave 90

Wave 90: derived Wave 99, private chief-of-staff drafting phase 8.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 99 asked to draft the private chief-of-staff pillar. The private cockpit already had next actions, task tracking, review sessions, briefing drafts, readiness, schedules, and priority reports. What was missing was a receipt-backed local-only draft packet layer that turns those pieces into review-only daily operating drafts without becoming email copy, calendar writes, applications, approvals, deployments, payments, or external instructions.

Changes:

- Added `data/private-chief-drafts.js` with:
  - `privateChiefDraftsPlan()`
  - local receipt read/write helpers
  - five lane-specific chief-of-staff draft packets
  - selected draft lookup via `selectPrivateChiefDraft()`
  - daily script generation for local review
  - explicit source traces from chief readiness, priorities, schedule blocks, briefing drafts, review sessions, approval queue, and outreach drafts
  - manual-only, review-required, non-sendable, no-external-write guardrails
  - checks for private route coverage, draft depth, priority linkage, schedule linkage, source trace coverage, manual-only guard, forbidden external actions, verification commands, receipt route coverage, and package script coverage
- Added `scripts/private-chief-drafts.mjs` and `npm run draft:private`, which starts a temporary localhost server with `ENABLE_PRIVATE_COCKPIT=1`, reads `/api/private/chief-of-staff/drafts`, and writes receipts to `var/private-chief-drafts-receipts.json`.
- Added gated private API routes:
  - `/api/private/chief-of-staff/drafts`
  - `/api/private/chief-of-staff/drafts/:id`
  - `/api/private/chief-of-staff/drafts/plan`
  - `/api/private/chief-of-staff/drafts/history`
- Embedded `chiefDrafts` into the local/private cockpit payload.
- Added private draft routes to the runtime route manifest and runtime-surface private gate probes.
- Added README docs, package script/check coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, private route gate coverage, runtime-surface coverage, and receipt coverage.
- Preserved the private boundary that draft packets are localhost/private planning artifacts only. They do not read inboxes, calendars, private documents, credentials, school portals, or external application systems, and they cannot send, schedule, submit, approve, deploy, spend, contact, or mark external outcomes as real.

- First `npm run draft:private` after implementation produced `private-chief-drafts-mposepws 10/10 check(s) passed; 5 draft(s); score 100/100; wrote var/private-chief-drafts-receipts.json`.

## Completed Wave 91

Wave 91: derived Wave 100, opportunity engine scorecard phase 8.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 100 asked to score the opportunity engine from public-safe evidence. The app already had an opportunity radar, packages, board gates, quality benchmarks, de-risking plans, and rankings. What was missing was a single receipt-backed scorecard layer that combines those signals into ranked 0-100 opportunity scorecards while keeping every output manual-only, proof-repair-oriented, and external-write-disabled.

Changes:

- Added `data/opportunity-scorecard.js` with:
  - `opportunityScorecardPlan()`
  - local receipt read/write helpers
  - opportunity scorecards scored across fit, readiness, ranking, quality, risk, proof depth, requirement coverage, manual safety, blocker penalties, and requirement penalties
  - score bands, dimensions, checks, repair queue, source traces, and selected scorecard lookup via `selectOpportunityScorecard()`
  - public-safe/manual-only policy text and forbidden actions for outreach, submissions, scheduling, publishing, deploying, and spending
- Added `scripts/opportunity-scorecard.mjs` and `npm run score:opportunities`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/opportunity-scorecard`, and writes receipts to `var/opportunity-scorecard-receipts.json`.
- Added public API routes:
  - `/api/opportunity-scorecard`
  - `/api/opportunity-scorecard/:id`
  - `/api/opportunity-scorecard/plan`
  - `/api/opportunity-scorecard/history`
- Added terminal command aliases:
  - `opportunity-scorecard`
  - `score-opportunities`
  - `opportunity-scoring`
- Added a visible `opportunity-scorecard` terminal shortcut.
- Added opportunity scorecard routes to the runtime route manifest and `/api/opportunity-scorecard` to the safe evidence refresh plan.
- Added runtime-surface sampling for `/api/opportunity-scorecard/:id` using `/api/opportunity-scorecard/agent-infra-internship` so the manifest probe uses a real opportunity scorecard id.
- Added README docs, package script/check coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, runtime-surface coverage, refresh coverage, terminal coverage, and receipt coverage.
- Preserved the source boundary that the scorecard reads only local public-safe opportunity, package, board, de-risking, ranking, quality, route, refresh, and receipt evidence. It does not claim live postings, deadlines, applications, interviews, scholarships, grants, funding, recipient interest, outreach status, or external availability.

Evidence:

- First `npm run score:opportunities` after implementation produced `opportunity-scorecard-mposwcsu 11/11 check(s) passed; 6 scorecard(s); top research-lab-accessibility; score 100/100; wrote var/opportunity-scorecard-receipts.json`.

- `node --test test/api-contract.test.mjs` passed after Wave 91: 9 tests, 1 suite, 0 failures.
- Runtime receipt refresh sequence:
  - `npm run build:inline` produced `Embedded 536792 bytes from command-center.mjs into index.html`
  - `npm run record:runtime` produced `runtime-mposx812 readiness 91/100; 4 runtime change(s); wrote var/runtime-truth-receipts.json`
  - Initial parallel `npm run record:runtime-surface` produced `surface-mposxedz 173/174 passed; 17/17 private gate probe(s); wrote var/runtime-surface-receipts.json`; inspection showed the generic `:id` sampler probed `/api/opportunity-scorecard/recruiter-vs-professor` and received `404`.
  - Patched the runtime-surface sampler to use `/api/opportunity-scorecard/agent-infra-internship`.
  - Rerun `npm run record:runtime-surface` produced `surface-mposyxt3 174/174 passed; 17/17 private gate probe(s); wrote var/runtime-surface-receipts.json`
  - `npm run refresh:evidence` produced `refresh-1779930943153 42/42 passed; wrote var/evidence-refresh-receipts.json`

## Completed Wave 92

Wave 92: derived Wave 101, runtime-truth diff phase 8.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 101 asked to diff runtime truth. The app already exposed raw runtime truth, fingerprint receipts, route-surface receipts, reconciliation, explanation, deploy-readiness, and evidence-chain reports. What was missing was a first-class diff report and recorder that classifies stable identity drift separately from volatile local runtime changes, ties that classification to route/refresh/script evidence, and makes the existing `runtime-diff` terminal command more useful than a raw fingerprint summary.

Changes:

- Added `data/runtime-diff.js` with:
  - `runtimeDiffPlan()`
  - local receipt read/write helpers
  - runtime-truth history window over recent fingerprint receipts
  - classified drift changes for identity versus volatile runtime fields
  - drift lanes for identity drift, volatile isolation, receipt currentness, route surface, refresh coverage, route manifest, script coverage, public/private boundary, and change classification
  - checks for fingerprint readiness, previous receipt history, change classification, identity guard, reconciliation freshness, surface freshness, route manifest coverage, refresh coverage, script coverage, and private-boundary safety
  - explicit non-claims for CDN cache, provider identity, uptime, private cockpit contents, dashboards, third-party account state, deploys, publishing, and external contacts
- Added `scripts/runtime-diff.mjs` and `npm run diff:runtime`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/runtime-diff`, and writes receipts to `var/runtime-diff-receipts.json`.
- Added public API routes:
  - `/api/runtime-diff`
  - `/api/runtime-diff/plan`
  - `/api/runtime-diff/history`
- Updated the existing `runtime-diff` terminal command to use the new report while preserving `fingerprint` as the raw runtime fingerprint command.
- Added runtime diff routes to the runtime route manifest.
- Added `/api/runtime-diff` to the safe evidence refresh plan.
- Added `/api/runtime-diff` to the runtime reconciliation truth-route ledger.
- Added README docs, package script/check coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, runtime-surface coverage, refresh coverage, terminal coverage, and receipt coverage.
- Preserved the public-safe runtime boundary. The diff report reads local public-safe runtime reports and receipts only; it does not deploy, publish, mutate git history, enable private routes, query provider dashboards, inspect private cockpit data, or contact third parties.

Evidence:

- Syntax checks passed for:
  - `node --check data/runtime-diff.js`
  - `node --check scripts/runtime-diff.mjs`
  - `node --check server.js`
  - `node --check scripts/validate-data.mjs`
- `node scripts/validate-data.mjs` passed after implementation: `Validated 13 projects, 74 claims, 77 artifacts, 13 checks, 41 private references.`
- Runtime receipt refresh sequence:

  - `npm run record:runtime-surface` produced `surface-mpotc2v1 177/177 passed; 17/17 private gate probe(s); wrote var/runtime-surface-receipts.json`
  - `npm run refresh:evidence` produced `refresh-1779931656244 43/43 passed; wrote var/evidence-refresh-receipts.json`
  - First `npm run diff:runtime` produced `runtime-diff-mpotcvr8 10/10 check(s) passed; identity 0; volatile 3; score 100/100; wrote var/runtime-diff-receipts.json`
- `npm run check` passed after implementation: `Validated 13 projects, 74 claims, 77 artifacts, 13 checks, 41 private references.`
- `node --test test/api-contract.test.mjs` passed after Wave 92: 9 tests, 1 suite, 0 failures.
- Downstream runtime receipts after adding runtime diff:
  - `npm run explain:runtime` produced `runtime-explain-mpotdmet 6/6 check(s) passed; 5 proof claim(s); score 100/100; wrote var/runtime-explain-receipts.json`
  - `npm run audit:runtime-deploy` produced `runtime-deploy-readiness-mpotdmeu 7/7 check(s) passed; 6/6 gate(s); score 100/100; wrote var/runtime-deploy-readiness-receipts.json`
  - `npm run audit:runtime-chain` produced `runtime-chain-mpotdmgm 12/12 check(s) passed; 7 link(s); score 100/100; wrote var/runtime-evidence-chain-receipts.json`
- `npm run verify` passed after Wave 92: check, inline build with `536792 bytes`, 9 API tests, and 2 Playwright E2E tests.

## Completed Wave 93

Wave 93: derived Wave 102, navigate design ambition phase 8.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 102 asked for a dense, accessible, mobile-safe, keyboard-first design ambition improvement. The command center already had keyboard shortcuts and skip links for search, terminal, and works, but the evidence-heavy ledger and graph panels were not first-class keyboard skip targets. The page also exposed an async-layout edge: early skip activation could update the hash before data-backed panels finished rendering, leaving the intended panel out of view.

Changes:

- Expanded the global skip-link nav in `index.html` from search/terminal/works to five targets: search, ledger, graph, terminal, and works.
- Added robust skip-link activation in `command-center.mjs`:
  - binds skip links before async data loading starts
  - prevents default fragment jumps
  - focuses the target section with `tabindex="-1"`
  - uses deterministic `scrollIntoView({ behavior: "auto", block: "start" })`
  - realigns the current skip target after `renderEverything()`, after `runGuide()`, after terminal/status hydration, and after constellation startup so async content cannot push the target out of view
- Updated keyboard-readiness source signals and checks to require five skip links, ledger and graph skip targets, and the runtime skip-link activation handler.
- Updated validator fixtures and API contract coverage for the five-link keyboard readiness summary.
- Updated Playwright E2E coverage so desktop keyboard navigation proves:
  - first Tab focuses `Skip to search`
  - second Tab focuses `Skip to ledger`, Enter updates `#command-ledger`, and the ledger is in the viewport
  - third Tab focuses `Skip to graph`, Enter updates `#command-graph`, and the graph is in the viewport
- Rebuilt the inline runtime bundle in `index.html` from `command-center.mjs`.
- Preserved the public-safe design boundary. The change only improves local UI navigation and evidence discoverability; it does not add credentials, private data access, production writes, external submissions, unsupported claims, or hidden routes.

Evidence:

- Syntax/data checks passed for:
  - `node --check command-center.mjs`
  - `node --check server.js`
  - `node --check data/keyboard-readiness.js`
  - `node --check scripts/validate-data.mjs`
  - `node scripts/validate-data.mjs` -> `Validated 13 projects, 74 claims, 77 artifacts, 13 checks, 41 private references.`
- Initial focused E2E caught the real bug: the ledger hash changed while `#command-ledger` remained outside the viewport. Investigation showed early keyboard activation could run before the async command-center layout settled. The source was patched rather than weakening the test.
- `npm run build:inline` after the final patch produced `Embedded 537444 bytes from command-center.mjs into index.html`.
- Focused `npm run test:e2e` passed after the final patch: 2 Playwright tests, 0 failures.
- Final `npm run audit:keyboard-readiness` produced `keyboard-readiness-mpoyn1r1 9/9 check(s) passed; 3 shortcut(s); score 100/100; wrote var/keyboard-readiness-receipts.json`.
- Final `npm run audit:design-stability` produced `design-stability-mpoyn1o5 8/8 check(s) passed; 29 shortcut(s); score 100/100; wrote var/design-stability-receipts.json`.
- Runtime receipt refresh sequence:
  - `npm run record:runtime` produced `runtime-mpoyn9fb readiness 91/100; 5 runtime change(s); wrote var/runtime-truth-receipts.json`
  - `npm run record:runtime-surface` produced `surface-mpoyni2i 177/177 passed; 17/17 private gate probe(s); wrote var/runtime-surface-receipts.json`
  - `npm run refresh:evidence` produced `refresh-1779940572232 43/43 passed; wrote var/evidence-refresh-receipts.json`
  - `npm run diff:runtime` produced `runtime-diff-mpoynxq0 10/10 check(s) passed; identity 0; volatile 3; score 100/100; wrote var/runtime-diff-receipts.json`
  - `npm run explain:runtime` produced `runtime-explain-mpoyo0lz 6/6 check(s) passed; 5 proof claim(s); score 100/100; wrote var/runtime-explain-receipts.json`
  - `npm run audit:runtime-deploy` produced `runtime-deploy-readiness-mpoyo3f3 7/7 check(s) passed; 6/6 gate(s); score 100/100; wrote var/runtime-deploy-readiness-receipts.json`
  - `npm run audit:runtime-chain` produced `runtime-chain-mpoyo619 12/12 check(s) passed; 7 link(s); score 100/100; wrote var/runtime-evidence-chain-receipts.json`
  - `npm run audit:design-ambition` produced `design-ambition-mpoyoay5 10/10 check(s) passed; 5/5 family/families; score 100/100; wrote var/design-ambition-receipts.json`
- Final `npm run verify` passed after Wave 93: check, inline build with `537444 bytes`, 9 API tests, and 2 Playwright E2E tests.
- Live API smoke on the running local server at `http://127.0.0.1:4173` returned:
  - `/api/keyboard-readiness 200 {"score":100,"checks":"9/9","skipLinks":5}`
  - `/api/design-ambition 200 {"score":100,"checks":"10/10"}`
  - `/api/runtime-diff 200 {"score":100,"checks":"10/10"}`
  - `/api/runtime-surface/latest 200 {"score":100}`
- Rendered Playwright smoke against `http://127.0.0.1:4173` returned:
  - ledger skip: `hash "#command-ledger"`, `activeId "command-ledger"`, viewport-intersecting rect `top 422`, `bottom 870`
  - graph skip: `hash "#command-graph"`, `activeId "command-graph"`, viewport-intersecting rect `top 26`, `bottom 1813`
  - `appIssues: []`
  - screenshots saved outside the repo at `/tmp/personal-skip-ledger.png` and `/tmp/personal-skip-graph.png`
- In-app Browser plugin was attempted for Wave 93 rendered validation, but the backend returned `Browser is not available: iab`; local Playwright was used as the browser fallback.

## Completed Wave 94

Wave 94: derived Wave 103, sample research-grade evaluation phase 8.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 103 asked for a repeatable public-safe evaluation sample. The app already had proof quality, search quality, opportunity quality, usability, stress, integrity, rigor, runtime, and design audits, but it lacked one deterministic sampler that picks representative items across those families and proves each sampled item remains falsifiable, source-traced, repairable, repeatable, route-covered, refresh-covered, and public-safe.

Changes:

- Added `data/evaluation-sample.js` with:
  - `evaluationSamplePlan()`
  - local receipt read/write helpers
  - deterministic samples across truthfulness, retrieval, opportunity gates, usability, runtime/meta-evaluation coherence, design, and route/refresh/script repeatability
  - sample matrix, repair queue, non-claims, source boundaries, side-effect boundaries, sampled item metadata, expected failure modes, repair actions, and verification commands
- Added `scripts/evaluation-sample.mjs` and `npm run sample:evaluation`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/evaluation/sample`, writes `var/evaluation-sample-receipts.json`, and fails when sampled checks fail.
- Added public API routes:
  - `/api/evaluation/sample`
  - `/api/evaluation/sample/plan`
  - `/api/evaluation/sample/history`
- Added the `evaluation-sample`, `sample-evaluation`, and `research-sample` terminal commands.
- Added evaluation sample routes to the runtime route manifest.
- Added `/api/evaluation/sample` to the safe evidence refresh plan.
- Added README docs, package script/check coverage, validator coverage, API contract coverage, runtime-surface coverage, refresh coverage, terminal coverage, and receipt coverage.
- Preserved the public-safe evaluation boundary. The sampler reads only local public-safe reports and receipts. It does not conduct live user research, collect analytics, read private cockpit data, use credentials, contact external services, deploy, publish, submit, approve, schedule, or mutate third-party systems.

- Syntax/data checks passed for:

- First `npm run sample:evaluation` after implementation produced `evaluation-sample-mpoz1upt 6/7 sample(s) passed; 7 domain(s); score 89/100; wrote var/evaluation-sample-receipts.json` and exited nonzero. Inspection showed a legitimate stale-evidence failure in `runtime-chain-sample`: runtime chain 62/100, research stress 86/100, evaluation integrity 85/100, research rigor 66/100. The sampler was not weakened; the upstream receipts were refreshed.

## Completed Wave 95

Wave 95: derived Wave 104, contrast narrative intelligence phase 8.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 104 called for narrative output generated or validated from evidence with uncertainty and repair guidance. The app already exposed `/api/narrative-contrast`, but it was only a static public-safe report. It lacked its own plan, receipt-backed recorder, history endpoint, refresh/runtime coverage, validator assertions, terminal receipt visibility, and proof that the contrast layer can be rerun like the newer narrative tailor/disclosure/sequence systems.

Changes:

- Upgraded `data/narrative-contrast.js` into a receipt-backed public-safe contrast intelligence report with:
  - `narrativeContrastPlan()`
  - local receipt read/write helpers
  - route, refresh, and package-script coverage checks
  - pairwise recruiter/professor/founder contrast checks
  - switchboard disclosure checks
  - repair queue, non-claims, methodology, latest receipt summary, side-effect boundary, and verification command
- Added `scripts/narrative-contrast.mjs` and `npm run contrast:narratives`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/narrative-contrast`, writes `var/narrative-contrast-receipts.json`, and fails on any report failure.
- Added public routes:
  - `/api/narrative-contrast/plan`
  - `/api/narrative-contrast/history`
- Updated the existing `narrative-contrast` terminal command to report latest receipt and next action.
- Added `/api/narrative-contrast` to the safe evidence refresh plan.
- Added contrast plan/history routes to the runtime route manifest.
- Updated README command/endpoint docs, package syntax coverage, validator coverage, and API contract coverage.
- Preserved the public-safe boundary. The contrast recorder only reads public-safe local narrative outputs and writes a local receipt. It does not publish copy, send outreach, submit applications, approve private artifacts, read private cockpit data, collect analytics, infer external interest, use credentials, deploy, or contact third parties.

## Completed Wave 96

Wave 96: derived Wave 105, quarantine evidence graph depth phase 9.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 105 asked for one verifiable relationship, confidence rule, or projection guard in the evidence graph. The app already had `/api/graph-projection-guard`, but it was a non-recorder guard and its quarantined edges were not first-class receipt-backed graph-depth evidence. This wave turned projection quarantine into an operational public-safe guard with plan/history routes, a runner, receipt history, and deeper quarantine metadata for graph confidence/depth consumers.

Changes:

- Upgraded `data/graph-projection-guard.js` with:
  - `graphProjectionGuardPlan()`
  - local receipt read/write helpers
  - route, refresh, and package-script coverage checks
  - a quarantine ledger grouped by relation family
  - highest-risk quarantine entries
  - severity counts, disposition counts, latest receipt summary, side-effect boundary, non-claims, repair actions, and verification command
- Added graph-depth disposition metadata to quarantined relationships:
  - `graphDepthDisposition`
  - `projectionState`
  - `severity`
  - `confidenceBand`
  - `manualReviewRequired`
  - `publicSafe`
- Added `scripts/graph-projection-guard.mjs` and `npm run audit:graph-guard`, which starts a temporary local server, reads `/api/graph-projection-guard`, writes `var/graph-projection-guard-receipts.json`, and fails on any guard failure.
- Added public routes:
  - `/api/graph-projection-guard/plan`
  - `/api/graph-projection-guard/history`
- Updated the existing `graph-guard` terminal command to report quarantine families and latest receipt.
- Added graph projection guard plan/history routes to the runtime route manifest.
- Updated README command/endpoint docs, package syntax coverage, validator coverage, and API contract coverage.
- Preserved the public-safe graph boundary. The guard reads only local public-safe graph, claim, artifact, and tailored narrative outputs and writes a local receipt. It does not publish, deploy, mutate graph storage, enable private routes, read private cockpit data, contact third parties, collect analytics, or infer external outcomes.

## Completed Wave 97

Wave 97: derived Wave 106, annotate artifact museum phase 9.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 106 asked to add or validate one public-safe artifact type, screenshot, transcript, replay, or gap record. The artifact museum already exposed captures, transcripts, replays, comparisons, and explicit screenshot gaps, but it lacked a curated annotation artifact that tells a reviewer or agent how to inspect a project safely without pretending screenshots or richer media exist.

Changes:

- Added a public-safe `curator-annotation` artifact type for every project in `data/artifact-model.js`.
- Each annotation includes:
  - public-safe museum URL and terminal command
  - `annotationPolicy`
  - display guidance tailored to the project domain
  - primary caveat
  - linked screenshot gap record
  - verification command
  - source trace through project record, museum route, museum command, and screenshot gap record
- Added `curatorAnnotations` counts to the artifact catalog and artifact museum summary.
- Added a `curator-annotations` artifact collection with curator notes and suggested inspection path.
- Added annotation readiness to the artifact museum score dimensions.
- Added a `curator-annotation-coverage` museum check.
- Adjusted transcript artifact priority so curator annotations can appear in generated transcript proof lines.
- Updated validator coverage and API contract coverage for catalog counts, annotation fields, collections, museum summary, museum check, and transcript visibility.
- Preserved the public-safe artifact boundary. The annotation artifact is a local museum note. It is not a screenshot, video, private file, endorsement, external usage claim, deployment proof, or third-party validation.

## Completed Wave 98

Wave 98: derived Wave 107, review private chief of staff phase 9.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 107 asked to improve local/private planning without exposing private documents or sending anything automatically. The private chief-of-staff readiness report already had lanes, checks, receipts, drafts, priorities, and private route gating, but it lacked a lane-by-lane local review board that makes each decision state, stop condition, and verification command explicit before a human acts.

Changes:

- Added a `local-private-chief-review-board` to `data/private-chief-of-staff.js`.
- Each review board item now includes:
  - lane ID, rank, score, priority, and objective
  - local input summaries only
  - review prompt
  - allowed local decisions: `reviewed-local-only`, `deferred`, `blocked`, `left-open`
  - explicit stop conditions for email, calendar, submissions, deployments, payments, raw private documents, and unverifiable actions
  - `manualOnly: true`
  - `externalWrite: false`
  - `publicProjectionAllowed: false`
  - verification command
- Added chief readiness summary counts for review board items and manual review gates.
- Added readiness checks:
  - `review-board-coverage`
  - `review-stop-conditions`
- Added the selected review board item to the daily operating plan.
- Updated validator and API contract model coverage for review board mode, manual-only gates, stop conditions, allowed decisions, checks, and today review item.
- Preserved the local/private boundary. The review board derives from existing local metadata and gated chief-of-staff outputs. It does not read inboxes, calendars, private documents, portals, credentials, or external systems, and it cannot send, schedule, submit, approve, deploy, purchase, or mutate third-party systems.

Evidence:

- Syntax/data checks passed for:
  - `node --check data/private-chief-of-staff.js`
  - `node --check scripts/validate-data.mjs`
  - `node --check server.js`
  - `node --check test/api-contract.test.mjs`
  - `node scripts/validate-data.mjs` -> `Validated 13 projects, 74 claims, 90 artifacts, 13 checks, 41 private references.`
- First private chief receipt after implementation:

## Completed Wave 99

Wave 99: derived Wave 108, package opportunity engine phase 9.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 108 asked to improve opportunity fit, requirements, missing proof, effort, or upside from public-safe evidence. The opportunity engine already had ranked packages, blockers, requirements, de-risking, quality, ranking, and scorecards, but the missing-proof repair work was scattered across blockers/checklists rather than packaged as an ordered, reusable, local-only repair plan.

Changes:

- Added first-class `packageReadiness` and `proofRepairPlan` fields to `data/opportunity-packages.js`.
- Each opportunity package now exposes:
  - requirement coverage totals, missing requirement counts, average proof score, repair step counts, effort/upside labels, manual-only state, no-external-write state, and live-posting/application-state boundaries
  - ordered proof repair steps derived from requirement gaps, de-risking checklist gaps, blockers, risk register entries, and weak evidence bundles
  - per-step priority, source, evidence, side effect, expected impact, verification command, and manual-use boundary
  - a `proof-repair-plan` source trace
- Updated opportunity quality evaluation to grade repair-plan actionability, include repair-plan counts in summaries/benchmarks, and add a `proof-repair-plan` quality check.
- Updated opportunity ranking so ranked rows and the global missing-proof queue consume package proof-repair steps first, including a package repair action summary and source trace.
- Updated opportunity scorecards so repair queues consume package proof-repair steps first, include repair-plan propagation as a scorecard dimension/check, and summarize propagated repair-plan volume.
- Updated the terminal `opportunity-package` command to show repair step counts, manual-only readiness state, and the first proof repair actions.
- Updated validators and API contract tests for package readiness, proof repair steps, quality/ranking/scorecard propagation, terminal output, and manual-only boundaries.
- Preserved the public-safe boundary. The feature does not ingest live postings, claim deadlines, submit applications, send outreach, schedule meetings, claim recipient interest, or write to third-party systems.

Evidence:

- Syntax checks passed for:
  - `node --check data/opportunity-packages.js`
  - `node --check data/opportunity-quality.js`
  - `node --check data/opportunity-ranking.js`
  - `node --check data/opportunity-scorecard.js`
  - `node --check scripts/validate-data.mjs`
  - `node --check server.js`

## Completed Wave 100

Wave 100: derived Wave 109, record runtime truth phase 9.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 109 asked for another local or deploy-aware runtime truth check with receipt/API/terminal coverage. Runtime truth already fingerprinted package, build, git, Node, domain, and volatile process identity, while route-surface, refresh, private-gate, and deploy-readiness checks lived downstream. That meant route/refresh/private-gate/script contract changes could be proven by receipts, but they were not part of the core runtime identity fingerprint.

Changes:

- Added a `surfaceContract` to the runtime truth response in `server.js`.
- The surface contract records:
  - public API route count
  - private API route count
  - static route count
  - evidence refresh endpoint count
  - private refresh endpoint count
  - private gate env/default status/localhost-only policy
  - critical runtime verification scripts
  - deploy comparison input categories
- Added surface-contract fields to the runtime truth identity fingerprint in `data/runtime-truth.js`, so route/refresh/private-gate/script contract changes now intentionally change the runtime identity hash.
- Added runtime checks:
  - `surface-contract`
  - `critical-script-contract`
- Extended runtime explanation with a `surface-contract` proof claim and quick-read line.
- Extended deploy readiness comparison packets with the same surface contract and added a deploy-readiness `surface-contract` check.
- Extended runtime evidence chain with a `runtime-surface-contract` link and `surface-contract-fingerprint` check.
- Updated terminal outputs:
  - `runtime` now shows the surface contract
  - `fingerprint` now shows the fingerprinted surface contract
  - `runtime-deploy` now shows the deploy comparison surface contract
- Updated validators and API contract tests for the new truth fields, checks, proof claim, deploy comparison packet, evidence-chain link, and terminal output.
- Preserved the public-safe boundary. The recorder still only starts a temporary local server, reads public-safe endpoints, writes local receipts, and does not deploy, publish, mutate git history, query provider dashboards, inspect CDN state, enable private cockpit data, or contact third parties.

Evidence:

- Syntax checks passed for:
  - `node --check server.js`
  - `node --check data/runtime-truth.js`
  - `node --check data/runtime-explain.js`
  - `node --check data/runtime-deploy-readiness.js`
  - `node --check data/runtime-evidence-chain.js`

  - `node --check test/api-contract.test.mjs`

## Completed Wave 101

Wave 101: derived Wave 110, adapt design ambition phase 9.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 110 asked to improve dense, accessible, mobile-safe, keyboard-first UI without hiding uncertainty. The command center already exposed proof health, a truth ledger, terminal shortcuts, keyboard navigation, and mobile overflow guards, but the proof ribbon itself was passive. The highest-leverage UI improvement was to make first-screen proof health actionable with native, keyboard-focusable command buttons that jump directly into the relevant terminal proof command.

Changes:

- Added proof-ribbon command buttons in `command-center.mjs`.
- Each proof-ribbon tile now exposes a native button:
  - claims -> `claim-calibration`
  - graph -> `graph-confidence`
  - runtime -> `runtime-chain`
  - opportunities -> `opportunity-scorecard`
- Clicking a proof-ribbon action scrolls to the terminal, fills the command input, and runs the command.
- Added aria labels for the proof-ribbon action buttons.
- Added stable proof-ribbon action geometry in `styles.css`: full-width native buttons, fixed minimum height, zero letter spacing, wrapping text, focus/hover treatment, and overflow guards.
- Updated command-center source signals to detect proof-ribbon actions, commands, labels, and geometry.
- Updated design stability:
  - summary `proofRibbonActions`
  - `proof-ribbon-actionability` check
  - `proof-ribbon-action-buttons` dense-control ledger entry
- Updated keyboard readiness:
  - summary `proofRibbonActions`
  - `proof-ribbon-actions` keyboard map entry
  - `proof-ribbon-actions` check
- Updated design ambition:
  - summary `proofRibbonActions`
  - proof-orientation family now requires proof-ribbon action buttons
  - first-screen proof compression check now requires four proof actions
- Updated API contract tests, validator fixtures/assertions, and Playwright E2E coverage.
- Rebuilt the inline runtime into `index.html`.
- Preserved uncertainty: the proof ribbon still surfaces needs-source, graph coverage, runtime probe, and missing-proof signals; the new buttons only make those signals easier to inspect.

  - `node --check command-center.mjs`

  - `node --check data/keyboard-readiness.js`

## Completed Wave 102

Wave 102: derived Wave 111, stress research-grade evaluation phase 9.

Rationale:

- Derived Wave 111 asked for another repeatable research-grade evaluation layer across truthfulness, usability, performance, accessibility, or proof quality. After Wave 101 made the first-screen proof ribbon actionable, the stress suite still did not fail if those proof-health signals became passive, unlabeled, or unreachable from keyboard/mobile-safe controls. The highest-leverage evaluator improvement was to make the research stress suite explicitly pressure-test first-screen proof actionability.

Changes:

- Extended `data/research-evaluation-stress.js` with design-system inputs:
  - `designStability`
  - `keyboardReadiness`
  - `designAmbition`
- Added a new high-severity stress scenario: `first-screen-proof-action-pressure`.
- The new scenario requires:
  - at least 4 proof-ribbon actions across design, keyboard, and ambition reports
  - passing design-stability `proof-ribbon-actionability`
  - passing keyboard-readiness `proof-ribbon-actions`
  - passing keyboard map entry for proof-ribbon actions
  - passing design-ambition `first-screen-proof-compression`
  - passing design-ambition `proof-orientation` family
  - design, keyboard, and ambition summary scores all at least 85
- Added stress summary fields:
  - `proofActionMinimum`
  - `proofActionScenarioPassing`
- Updated the research stress plan to list `first-screen proof action pressure`.
- Wired `currentResearchEvaluationStressReport` in `server.js` to pass current design-stability, keyboard-readiness, and design-ambition reports.
- Updated terminal `research-stress` output to show proof-action coverage and proof-action scenario status.
- Updated validator fixtures/assertions and API contract tests for the new scenario and summary fields.
- Preserved public-safety boundaries: the scenario reads only public-safe local reports and receipts; it does not expose private cockpit data, contact third parties, submit anything, or claim human usability research.
