## Completed Wave 151

Wave 151: convert `/api/change-history` into a latest-snapshot-backed fast path with explicit refresh.

Rationale:

- `route-latency-mppopen4` identified `/api/change-history` as the slowest remaining measured route at `4803ms`.
- Change history uses a snapshot store rather than a receipt store, and the old recorder wrote a lightweight snapshot that lacked graph-lineage proof-repair paths.
- The public route now serves the latest stored local snapshot by default, while `?refresh=1` recomputes the current graph-aware snapshot without recording it.

Changes:

- Updated `data/change-history.js`:
  - Adds endpoint/history metadata, cache policy, side-effect boundary, and refresh endpoint to the plan/report.
  - Adds `buildChangeHistoryReportFromSnapshots()` for latest-stored-snapshot reads.
  - Marks live reports with `cachedFromSnapshot: false` and cached reports with `cachedFromSnapshot: true`.
- Updated `scripts/change-history.mjs`:
  - The recorder now starts a temporary server and fetches `/api/change-history?refresh=1`, so recorded snapshots include the same graph-aware artifact-gap repair metrics as the live app.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - `/api/change-history` now serves the latest stored snapshot by default when one exists.
  - `/api/change-history?refresh=1` forces full recomputation.
  - Terminal `changes` uses the latest stored snapshot when available.
  - Added `/api/change-history/history`.
- Updated route/docs/test coverage:
  - Added `/api/change-history/history` to the runtime route manifest and `README.md`.
  - Updated validation and API tests for cache flags, refresh endpoint, history endpoint, and cached snapshot shape.

Rationale:

- `route-latency-mppp24cw` identified `/api/graph-confidence` as the slowest remaining measured route at `3464ms`.
- Graph confidence already had a receipt store, but the receipt only kept a top sample of relationships, all from the same family.
- This wave upgraded the receipt to store the full public-safe relationship list so the cached route preserves crosslink, disclosure, and projection coverage.

  - Validates live and cached graph-confidence report shapes.
  - Asserts the public route exposes cache flags and refresh endpoint.
  - Validates cached relationships preserve crosslink, disclosure, and projection families.

Wave 154: convert `/api/opportunity-scorecard`, `/api/opportunity-scorecard/:id`, and terminal `opportunity-scorecard` into receipt-backed fast paths with explicit refresh.

Rationale:

- `route-latency-mpppptan` identified `/api/opportunity-scorecard/agent-infra-internship` at `5620ms`, `/api/opportunity-scorecard` at `5406ms`, and terminal `opportunity-scorecard` at `3900ms`.
- Scorecard receipts already existed, but only stored top rows and could not reconstruct selected scorecards with gates, source traces, and repair actions.
- This wave upgraded scorecard receipts to store the full public-safe scorecard set and made both collection and selected routes cached by default.

Changes:

- Updated `data/opportunity-scorecard.js`:
  - Adds `cachedFromReceipt`, `cachePolicy`, and `refreshEndpoint` to live reports.
  - Adds `buildOpportunityScorecardReportFromReceipt()` to reconstruct collection and selected scorecards from the latest local receipt.
  - Preserves decision gates, manual-only boundaries, source traces, risk profiles, evidence profiles, repair actions, score bands, dimensions, and repair queue.
- Updated `scripts/opportunity-scorecard.mjs`:
  - `npm run score:opportunities` now calls `/api/opportunity-scorecard?refresh=1`.
  - Receipts now store full scorecards, not only top summary rows.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - `/api/opportunity-scorecard` now serves the latest receipt-backed report by default when one exists.
  - `/api/opportunity-scorecard?refresh=1` forces full recomputation.
  - `/api/opportunity-scorecard/:id` uses the same cached-default/explicit-refresh split.
  - Terminal `opportunity-scorecard` uses the receipt-backed report when available.
- Updated validation and API tests:
  - Validates live and cached scorecard report shapes.
  - Asserts collection and selected scorecard routes expose cache flags and refresh endpoints.
  - Verifies cached selected scorecards preserve ranking source traces.

## Completed Wave 166

Wave 166: convert `/api/status` into a receipt-backed default read while preserving live status receipt-writing behind explicit record/refresh paths.

Rationale:

- `route-latency-mppstwet` had no slow frontiers, but its heatmap identified `/api/status` as the slowest measured route at `315ms`.
- `/api/status` previously recomputed internal route, configured domain, live demo, and GitHub profile checks and appended a receipt on every GET.
- This wave made ordinary status reads side-effect-light and fast by serving the latest local receipt, while keeping live network checks and receipt-writing available through `/api/status?refresh=1`, `/api/status?record=1`, and `npm run record:status`.

Changes:

- Updated `server.js`:
  - `/api/status` now returns the latest local status receipt by default when one exists.
  - `/api/status?refresh=1` and `/api/status?record=1` run live checks and write a new local status receipt.
  - Default status responses expose `cachedFromReceipt`, `cachePolicy`, `refreshEndpoint`, `recordEndpoint`, `receiptRecorded`, `sourceBoundary`, and `sideEffectBoundary`.
  - Added `/api/status/plan`.
- Updated `data/verification-receipts.js`:
  - Adds `statusPlan()`.
  - New receipts include `mode`, source/side-effect boundaries, and `verificationCommand`.
- Added `scripts/status.mjs` and `npm run record:status` as the repo-native explicit status recorder.
- Updated `data/runtime-attestation.js` and `README.md` to declare `/api/status/plan`.
- Updated API tests to assert cached default status behavior, explicit record behavior, and the status plan.

- Runtime evidence chain receipts existed, but they were thin and omitted endpoint, non-claim, evidence, tripwire, repair, and verification fields needed to reconstruct the public contract.
- This wave made default reads cached by receipt only after a full-fidelity receipt exists, while preserving live recomputation through `/api/runtime-evidence-chain?refresh=1` and `npm run audit:runtime-chain`.

  - Adds live cache metadata: `cachedFromReceipt: false`, `cachePolicy: live-refresh`, and `refreshEndpoint: /api/runtime-evidence-chain?refresh=1`.
  - Adds `latestReceipt` metadata to live reports.
  - Adds `buildRuntimeEvidenceChainReportFromReceipt()` with strict guards so old thin receipts fall back to live recompute instead of being trusted.
  - Reconstructs cached custody packets, chain links, parity tripwires, receipt matrix, checks, repair actions, non-claims, next action, and verification command from full receipts.
- Updated `scripts/runtime-evidence-chain.mjs`:
  - Recorder now calls `/api/runtime-evidence-chain?refresh=1`.
  - Receipts now store full chain link, parity tripwire, check, receipt-matrix, repair-action, non-claim, source-boundary, and verification evidence.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - `/api/runtime-evidence-chain` now serves the latest full receipt-backed report by default.
  - `/api/runtime-evidence-chain?refresh=1` forces live recomputation.
  - Terminal `runtime-chain` uses the receipt-backed report when available and names the receipt id.
- Updated `scripts/validate-data.mjs` and `test/api-contract.test.mjs`:
  - Validates live and cached runtime-evidence-chain report shapes.
  - Asserts cache flags, refresh endpoint, and live refresh behavior.

- `route-latency-mppu276a` had no slow frontiers, but its heatmap identified `/api/graph-scoreboard` as the slowest measured route at `137ms`.
- Graph scoreboard receipts existed, but they were lossy and omitted the normalization ledger, quarantine items, repair action reasons, methodology, crosslink coverage, and route contract details needed to replay the API contract.
- This wave made default scoreboard reads cached by full report receipt only after a new full receipt exists, while old lossy receipts fall back to live recompute.

  - Adds live cache metadata: `cachedFromReceipt: false`, `cachePolicy: live-refresh`, and `refreshEndpoint: /api/graph-scoreboard?refresh=1`.
  - Adds `sideEffectBoundary` and `latestReceipt` metadata to live reports.
  - Adds `buildGraphScoreboardFromReceipt()` to replay a full public-safe graph-scoreboard report snapshot from the latest receipt.
  - Rejects old lossy receipts that do not contain the full report snapshot.
- Updated `scripts/graph-scoreboard.mjs`:
  - Recorder now calls `/api/graph-scoreboard?refresh=1`.
  - Receipts now store the full scoreboard report, full dimensions, full checks, full repair-action samples, and the public-safe side-effect boundary.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - `/api/graph-scoreboard` now serves the latest full receipt-backed report by default.
  - `/api/graph-scoreboard?refresh=1` forces live recomputation.
  - Terminal `graph-scoreboard` uses the receipt-backed report when available and names the receipt id.
- Updated `scripts/validate-data.mjs` and `test/api-contract.test.mjs`:
  - Validates live and cached graph-scoreboard report shapes.
  - Asserts cache flags, refresh endpoint, and live refresh behavior.

## Completed Wave 171

Wave 171: add a receipt-backed public `/api/graph` snapshot with explicit refresh while keeping internal graph consumers live.

Rationale:

- `route-latency-mppucaq7` had no slow frontiers, but its heatmap identified `/api/graph` as the slowest measured route at `124ms`.
- `/api/graph` had no receipt store at all and rebuilt the full public graph projection on every public route and terminal read.
- Internal graph derivative reports still need live graph data when they record their own receipts, so this wave added a public-route snapshot wrapper without changing the raw `graphPayload()` fan-out used by graph-quality, scoreboard, lineage, projection guard, and depth reports.

Changes:

- Added `data/graph-snapshot.js`:
  - Provides `graphSnapshotPlan()`, `buildGraphPayloadSnapshot()`, `buildGraphPayloadFromReceipt()`, `appendGraphSnapshotReceipt()`, and `readGraphSnapshotReceipts()`.
  - Stores receipts under `var/graph-snapshot-receipts.json`.
  - Validates cached snapshots for required public graph node families and edge relations before replaying them.
  - Adds live/cache metadata: `cachedFromReceipt`, `cachePolicy`, `refreshEndpoint`, `summary`, `latestReceipt`, source boundary, side-effect boundary, and plan.
- Added `scripts/graph-snapshot.mjs`:
  - Records `/api/graph?refresh=1` into a full public-safe snapshot receipt.
  - Fails if the graph shape does not contain the public-safe node/edge families required by the app.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - Added `currentGraphPayload({ preferReceipt })`.
  - `/api/graph` now serves the latest snapshot-backed graph by default.
  - `/api/graph?refresh=1` forces live graph projection.
  - Added `/api/graph/plan` and `/api/graph/history`.
  - Terminal `graph` now uses the snapshot-backed report when available and names the receipt id.
  - Left raw `graphPayload()` live for internal derivative reports to avoid stale receipt feedback loops.
- Updated `package.json`, `data/runtime-attestation.js`, `data/evidence-refresh.js`, `README.md`, `scripts/validate-data.mjs`, and `test/api-contract.test.mjs` for the new recorder, manifest entries, refresh coverage, docs, validation, and API contract.

- Targeted syntax checks passed for:
  - `data/graph-snapshot.js`
  - `scripts/graph-snapshot.mjs`
  - `server.js`

  - `data/evidence-refresh.js`
  - `scripts/validate-data.mjs`
  - `test/api-contract.test.mjs`

- `npm run record:graph-snapshot` forced the full refresh route and produced `graph-snapshot-mppunb0o 460 node(s), 651 edge(s), 23 node type(s), 36 relation(s); wrote var/graph-snapshot-receipts.json`
- Receipt shape check confirmed:

## Completed Wave 172

Wave 172: convert `/api/graph-quality` and terminal `graph-quality` into receipt-backed fast paths with explicit refresh.

Rationale:

- `route-latency-mppuqnfd` had no slow frontiers, but its heatmap identified `/api/graph-quality` as the slowest measured route at `96ms`.
- Graph-quality receipts existed, but they were lossy and omitted the full report, repair actions, source boundary, and check verification commands needed to replay the public contract.
- This wave made default graph-quality reads cached by full report receipt only after a new rich receipt exists, while old lossy receipts fall back to live recompute.

Changes:

- Updated `data/graph-quality.js`:
  - Adds live cache metadata: `cachedFromReceipt: false`, `cachePolicy: live-refresh`, and `refreshEndpoint: /api/graph-quality?refresh=1`.
  - Adds `sideEffectBoundary` and `latestReceipt` metadata to live reports.
  - Adds `buildGraphQualityReportFromReceipt()` to replay a full public-safe graph-quality report snapshot from the latest receipt.
  - Rejects old lossy receipts that do not contain the full report snapshot.
- Updated `scripts/graph-quality.mjs`:
  - Recorder now calls `/api/graph-quality?refresh=1`.
  - Receipts now store the full graph-quality report, full checks, gaps, repair actions, source boundary, and side-effect boundary.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - `/api/graph-quality` now serves the latest full receipt-backed report by default.
  - `/api/graph-quality?refresh=1` forces live recomputation.
  - Terminal `graph-quality` uses the receipt-backed report when available and names the receipt id.
  - Internal graph-quality consumers keep defaulting to live data.
- Updated `scripts/validate-data.mjs` and `test/api-contract.test.mjs`:
  - Validates live and cached graph-quality report shapes.
  - Asserts cache flags, refresh endpoint, and live refresh behavior.

Wave 173: convert `/api/graph-lineage` and terminal `graph-lineage` into receipt-backed fast paths with explicit refresh.

Rationale:

- `route-latency-mppv0wox` had no slow frontiers, but its heatmap identified `/api/graph-lineage` as the slowest measured route at `100ms`.
- Graph-lineage receipts existed, but they were summary-only and omitted the full audience lineage, artifact gap repair lineage, checks, unresolved evidence, repair actions, methodology, and source boundary needed to replay the public contract.
- This wave made default graph-lineage reads cached by full report receipt only after a new rich receipt exists, while old summary-only receipts fall back to live recompute.

  - Internal graph-lineage aggregation consumers keep defaulting to live data.
- Updated `scripts/validate-data.mjs` and `test/api-contract.test.mjs`:
  - Validates live and cached graph-lineage report shapes.
  - Asserts cache flags, refresh endpoint, and live refresh behavior.

Claude offload:

- Used `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions` as a read-only scout for the next target.
- Claude identified `/api/evaluation/claim-calibration` as the next heatmap target and confirmed the key implementation hazard: existing receipts are thin and must be rejected until a full calibration report receipt is recorded through `?refresh=1`.

Wave 175: convert `/api/evaluation/claim-calibration` and terminal `claim-calibration` into receipt-backed fast paths with explicit refresh.

Rationale:

- `route-latency-mppvm5dn` had no slow frontiers, but its heatmap identified `/api/evaluation/claim-calibration` as the slowest measured route at `80ms`.
- Existing claim-calibration receipts were thin: they preserved summary data, abbreviated checks, and truncated repair queue entries, but omitted the full report, calibration matrix, calibration policy, source boundary, non-claims, next action, and verification command.
- This wave made default claim-calibration reads cached by full report receipt only after a rich receipt exists, while old lossy receipts are rejected and fall back to live recompute.

Changes:

- Updated `data/claim-calibration.js`:
  - Adds live cache metadata: `cachedFromReceipt: false`, `cachePolicy: live-refresh`, and `refreshEndpoint: /api/evaluation/claim-calibration?refresh=1`.
  - Uses the recorder plan's public-safe side-effect boundary on live reports.
  - Adds `latestReceipt` metadata to live reports.
  - Adds `buildClaimCalibrationReportFromReceipt()` to strictly replay full claim-calibration report snapshots from rich receipts.
  - Rejects old thin receipts that do not contain the embedded full report, checks, calibration policy, calibration matrix, repair queue, and non-claims.
- Updated `scripts/claim-calibration.mjs`:
  - Recorder now calls `/api/evaluation/claim-calibration?refresh=1`.
  - Receipts now store the full report plus plan, checks, calibration policy, calibration matrix, full repair queue, source boundary, side-effect boundary, non-claims, next action, verification command, and generated timestamp.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - `/api/evaluation/claim-calibration` now serves the latest full receipt-backed report by default.
  - `/api/evaluation/claim-calibration?refresh=1` forces live recomputation.
  - Terminal `claim-calibration` uses the receipt-backed report when available and names the receipt id.
  - Internal claim-calibration consumers keep defaulting to live data.
- Updated `scripts/validate-data.mjs` and `test/api-contract.test.mjs`:
  - Validates live and cached claim-calibration report shapes.
  - Asserts cache flags, refresh endpoint, cached fixture replay, thin receipt rejection, and live refresh behavior.

Wave 176: convert `/api/runtime-deploy-readiness` and terminal `runtime-deploy` into receipt-backed fast paths with explicit refresh.

Rationale:

- `route-latency-mppvyalt` had no slow frontiers, but its heatmap identified `/api/runtime-deploy-readiness` as the slowest measured route at `54ms`.
- Existing runtime-deploy-readiness receipts were lossy: they preserved summary data, abbreviated gates, a reduced comparison packet, and abbreviated checks, but omitted the full report, gate labels, repair actions, verification commands, full surface contract, handoff checklist, non-claims, repair actions, next action, and verification command.
- This wave made default deploy-readiness reads cached by full report receipt only after a rich receipt exists, while old lossy receipts are rejected and fall back to live recompute.

Changes:

- Updated `data/runtime-deploy-readiness.js`:
  - Adds live cache metadata: `cachedFromReceipt: false`, `cachePolicy: live-refresh`, and `refreshEndpoint: /api/runtime-deploy-readiness?refresh=1`.
  - Adds `latestReceipt` metadata to live reports.
  - Adds `buildRuntimeDeployReadinessReportFromReceipt()` to strictly replay full runtime-deploy-readiness report snapshots from rich receipts.
  - Rejects old thin receipts that do not contain the embedded full report, full gates, full comparison packet, handoff checklist, checks, non-claims, and next action.
- Updated `scripts/runtime-deploy-readiness.mjs`:
  - Recorder now calls `/api/runtime-deploy-readiness?refresh=1`.
  - Receipts now store the full report plus plan, gates, comparison packet, handoff checklist, checks, non-claims, repair actions, source boundary, side-effect boundary, next action, verification command, and generated timestamp.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - `/api/runtime-deploy-readiness` now serves the latest full receipt-backed report by default.
  - `/api/runtime-deploy-readiness?refresh=1` forces live recomputation.
  - Terminal `runtime-deploy` uses the receipt-backed report when available and names the receipt id.
  - Internal runtime-deploy-readiness consumers keep defaulting to live data.
- Updated `scripts/validate-data.mjs` and `test/api-contract.test.mjs`:
  - Validates live and cached runtime-deploy-readiness report shapes.
  - Asserts cache flags, refresh endpoint, cached fixture replay, thin receipt rejection, and live refresh behavior.

Wave 177: convert `/api/runtime-explain` and terminal `runtime-explain` into receipt-backed fast paths with explicit refresh.

Rationale:

- `route-latency-mppw57jd` had no slow frontiers, but its heatmap identified `/api/runtime-explain` as the slowest measured route at `59ms`.
- Existing runtime-explain receipts were lossy: they preserved summary data, claim-firewall counts, audit-ladder counts, slim proof-claim rows, and slim checks, but omitted the full explainer report, quick read, identity explanation, receipt explanations, route/boundary explanations, blocked claim bodies, audit ladder steps, proof statements/limits/evidence, non-claims, repair actions, next action, and verification command.
- This wave made default runtime-explain reads cached by full report receipt only after a rich receipt exists, while old lossy receipts are rejected and fall back to live recompute.

Changes:

- Updated `data/runtime-explain.js`:
  - Adds live cache metadata: `cachedFromReceipt: false`, `cachePolicy: live-refresh`, and `refreshEndpoint: /api/runtime-explain?refresh=1`.
  - Adds `latestReceipt` and `verificationCommand` metadata to live reports.
  - Adds `buildRuntimeExplanationReportFromReceipt()` to strictly replay full runtime-explain report snapshots from rich receipts.
  - Rejects old thin receipts that do not contain the embedded full report, receipt explanations, claim firewall, audit ladder, proof claims, checks, and non-claims.
- Updated `scripts/runtime-explain.mjs`:
  - Recorder now calls `/api/runtime-explain?refresh=1`.
  - Receipts now store the full report plus plan, quick read, identity explanation, receipt explanations, route explanation, boundary explanation, claim firewall, audit ladder, proof claims, checks, non-claims, repair actions, source boundary, side-effect boundary, next action, verification command, and generated timestamp.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - `/api/runtime-explain` now serves the latest full receipt-backed report by default.
  - `/api/runtime-explain?refresh=1` forces live recomputation.
  - Terminal `runtime-explain` uses the receipt-backed report when available and names the receipt id.
  - Internal runtime-evidence-chain consumers keep defaulting to live runtime-explain data.
- Updated `scripts/validate-data.mjs` and `test/api-contract.test.mjs`:
  - Validates live and cached runtime-explain report shapes.
  - Asserts cache flags, refresh endpoint, cached fixture replay, thin receipt rejection, and live refresh behavior.

Claude offload:

- Used `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions` as a read-only scout for the runtime-explain conversion.

Wave 178: convert `/api/runtime-diff` and terminal `runtime-diff` into receipt-backed fast paths with explicit refresh.

Rationale:

- `route-latency-mppwc47b` had no slow frontiers, but its heatmap identified `/api/runtime-diff` as the slowest measured route at `47ms`.
- Existing runtime-diff receipts were lossy: they preserved summary data, current/previous hashes, slim lane rows, and slim checks, but omitted the full report, drift matrix, history window details, lane labels/bands/verification commands, check repair actions, non-claims, next action, and verification command.
- This wave made default runtime-diff reads cached by full report receipt only after a rich receipt exists, while old lossy receipts are rejected and fall back to live recompute.

Changes:

- Updated `data/runtime-diff.js`:
  - Adds live cache metadata: `cachedFromReceipt: false`, `cachePolicy: live-refresh`, and `refreshEndpoint: /api/runtime-diff?refresh=1`.
  - Adds `latestReceipt` metadata to live reports.
  - Adds `buildRuntimeDiffReportFromReceipt()` to strictly replay full runtime-diff report snapshots from rich receipts.
  - Rejects old thin receipts that do not contain the embedded full report, drift matrix, lanes, history window, checks, non-claims, next action, and verification command.
- Updated `scripts/runtime-diff.mjs`:
  - Recorder now calls `/api/runtime-diff?refresh=1`.
  - Receipts now store the full report plus plan, current/previous runtime rows, drift matrix, lanes, history window, checks, non-claims, repair actions, source boundary, side-effect boundary, next action, verification command, and generated timestamp.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - `/api/runtime-diff` now serves the latest full receipt-backed report by default.
  - `/api/runtime-diff?refresh=1` forces live recomputation.
  - Terminal `runtime-diff` uses the receipt-backed report when available and names the receipt id.
  - Internal runtime consumers keep defaulting to live runtime-diff data.
- Updated `scripts/validate-data.mjs` and `test/api-contract.test.mjs`:
  - Validates live and cached runtime-diff report shapes.
  - Asserts cache flags, refresh endpoint, cached fixture replay, thin receipt rejection, and live refresh behavior.

Wave 179: convert `/api/graph-crosslinks` and terminal `graph-links` into receipt-backed fast paths with explicit refresh.

Rationale:

- `route-latency-mppwkfxy` had no slow frontiers, but its heatmap identified `/api/graph-crosslinks` as the slowest measured route at `48ms`.
- Existing graph-crosslink receipts were lossy: they preserved summary data, relation types, slim checks, and only `12` sample links, but omitted the full crosslink set, projection guard, repair actions, next action, verification command, source boundary, and cache metadata.
- This wave made default graph-crosslink reads cached by full report receipt only after a rich receipt exists, while old lossy receipts are rejected and fall back to live recompute.

Changes:

- Updated `data/graph-crosslinks.js`:
  - Adds live cache metadata: `cachedFromReceipt: false`, `cachePolicy: live-refresh`, and `refreshEndpoint: /api/graph-crosslinks?refresh=1`.
  - Adds `sideEffectBoundary`, `latestReceipt`, `nextAction`, and `verificationCommand` metadata to live reports.
  - Adds `buildGraphCrosslinkReportFromReceipt()` to strictly replay full graph-crosslink report snapshots from rich receipts.
  - Rejects old thin receipts that do not contain the embedded full report, relation types, checks, full crosslinks, projection guard, and repair actions.
- Updated `scripts/graph-crosslinks.mjs`:
  - Recorder now calls `/api/graph-crosslinks?refresh=1`.
  - Receipts now store the full report plus plan, relation types, checks, all crosslinks, projection guard, repair actions, source boundary, side-effect boundary, next action, verification command, and generated timestamp.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - `/api/graph-crosslinks` now serves the latest full receipt-backed report by default.
  - `/api/graph-crosslinks?refresh=1` forces live recomputation.
  - Terminal `graph-links` uses the receipt-backed report when available and names the receipt id.
  - Internal graph consumers keep defaulting to live graph-crosslink data.
- Updated `scripts/validate-data.mjs` and `test/api-contract.test.mjs`:
  - Validates live and cached graph-crosslink report shapes.
  - Asserts cache flags, refresh endpoint, cached fixture replay, thin receipt rejection, and live refresh behavior.

Wave 180: add a runtime-reconciliation recorder and convert `/api/runtime-reconciliation` plus terminal `runtime-reconciliation` into receipt-backed fast paths with explicit refresh.

Rationale:

- `route-latency-mppws98w` had no slow frontiers, but its heatmap identified `/api/runtime-reconciliation` as the slowest measured route at `48ms`.
- Runtime reconciliation had no dedicated recorder or receipt store, so there was no safe cached source to replay from.
- This wave added a full local reconciliation recorder, then made default runtime-reconciliation reads cached by full report receipt only after a rich receipt exists.

Changes:

- Updated `data/runtime-reconciliation.js`:
  - Adds `runtimeReconciliationPlan()`, local receipt storage, append/read helpers, and `buildRuntimeReconciliationReportFromReceipt()`.
  - Adds live cache metadata: `cachedFromReceipt: false`, `cachePolicy: live-refresh`, and `refreshEndpoint: /api/runtime-reconciliation?refresh=1`.
  - Adds `plan`, `latestReceipt`, and `latestReceiptId` metadata to live reports.
  - Strictly replays full reconciliation snapshots and rejects thin or malformed receipts.
- Added `scripts/runtime-reconciliation.mjs`:
  - Starts a temporary local server.
  - Fetches `/api/runtime-reconciliation?refresh=1`.
  - Writes full receipts to `var/runtime-reconciliation-receipts.json`.
- Updated `package.json`:
  - Adds `audit:runtime-reconciliation`.
  - Adds `node --check scripts/runtime-reconciliation.mjs` to `npm run check`.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - `/api/runtime-reconciliation` now serves the latest full receipt-backed report by default.
  - `/api/runtime-reconciliation?refresh=1` forces live recomputation.
  - Terminal `runtime-reconciliation` uses the receipt-backed report when available and names the receipt id.
  - Internal runtime consumers keep defaulting to live reconciliation data.
- Updated `scripts/validate-data.mjs` and `test/api-contract.test.mjs`:
  - Validates live and cached reconciliation report shapes.
  - Asserts cache flags, refresh endpoint, cached fixture replay, thin receipt rejection, and live refresh behavior.

## Wave 181 - Opportunity Board Receipt Cache

Goal:

- Continue beyond the empty slow frontier by optimizing the next heatmap hint, `/api/opportunity-board`, with a receipt-backed cached default and explicit refresh.

Changes:

- Updated `data/opportunity-board.js`:
  - Adds `opportunityBoardPlan()`, local receipt storage, append/read helpers, and `buildOpportunityBoardFromReceipt()`.
  - Adds live cache metadata: `cachedFromReceipt: false`, `cachePolicy: live-refresh`, and `refreshEndpoint: /api/opportunity-board?refresh=1`.
  - Adds `plan`, `sideEffectBoundary`, `latestReceipt`, and `latestReceiptId` metadata to board reports.
  - Strictly replays full board snapshots and rejects thin or malformed receipts.
- Added `scripts/opportunity-board.mjs`:
  - Starts a temporary local server.
  - Fetches `/api/opportunity-board?refresh=1`.
  - Writes full receipts to `var/opportunity-board-receipts.json`.
- Updated `package.json`:
  - Adds `audit:opportunity-board`.
  - Adds `node --check scripts/opportunity-board.mjs` to `npm run check`.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - `/api/opportunity-board` now serves the latest full receipt-backed board by default.
  - `/api/opportunity-board?refresh=1` forces live recomputation.
  - Terminal `opportunity-board` uses the receipt-backed board when available and names the receipt id.
  - Internal opportunity consumers keep defaulting to live board data.
- Updated `scripts/validate-data.mjs` and `test/api-contract.test.mjs`:
  - Validates live and cached board shapes.
  - Asserts cache flags, refresh endpoint, plan metadata, cached fixture replay, thin receipt rejection, and live refresh behavior.

## Wave 182 - Opportunity Quality Rich Receipt Cache

Goal:

- Continue beyond the empty slow frontier by optimizing `/api/evaluation/opportunity-quality`, the next heatmap hint, by replacing thin receipts and live default reads with strict rich receipt replay plus explicit `?refresh=1` recomputation.

Changes:

- Updated `data/opportunity-quality.js`:
  - Adds live cache metadata: `cachedFromReceipt: false`, `cachePolicy: live-refresh`, and `refreshEndpoint: /api/evaluation/opportunity-quality?refresh=1`.
  - Adds `sideEffectBoundary` and `latestReceipt` metadata to live reports.
  - Adds `buildOpportunityQualityEvaluationFromReceipt()` with strict full-report validation.
  - Rejects old thin receipts and malformed receipts instead of serving incomplete cached quality output.
- Updated `scripts/opportunity-quality.mjs`:
  - Fetches `/api/evaluation/opportunity-quality?refresh=1` so the recorder cannot self-cache stale default route data.
  - Writes a full quality report snapshot into `var/opportunity-quality-receipts.json`.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - `/api/evaluation/opportunity-quality` now serves the latest full receipt-backed quality report by default.
  - `/api/evaluation/opportunity-quality?refresh=1` forces live recomputation.
  - Terminal `opportunity-quality` uses the receipt-backed quality report when available and names the receipt id.
  - Internal opportunity consumers keep defaulting to live quality data.
- Updated `scripts/validate-data.mjs` and `test/api-contract.test.mjs`:
  - Validates live and cached quality report shapes.
  - Asserts cache flags, refresh endpoint, cached fixture replay, thin receipt rejection, and live refresh behavior.

## Wave 183 - Narrative Objections Receipt Cache

Goal:

- Continue beyond the empty slow frontier by optimizing `/api/narrative-objections`, the next heatmap hint, by converting it from a live-only report into a receipt-backed cached default with explicit `?refresh=1` recomputation.

Changes:

- Updated `data/narrative-objections.js`:
  - Adds `narrativeObjectionPlan()`, local receipt storage, append/read helpers, and `buildNarrativeObjectionReportFromReceipt()`.
  - Adds live cache metadata: `cachedFromReceipt: false`, `cachePolicy: live-refresh`, and `refreshEndpoint: /api/narrative-objections?refresh=1`.
  - Adds `plan`, `sideEffectBoundary`, `repairActions`, `latestReceipt`, `latestReceiptId`, and `verificationCommand` metadata.
  - Adds check-level repair actions and strict full-report receipt validation.
  - Rejects thin or malformed receipts.
- Added `scripts/narrative-objections.mjs`:
  - Starts a temporary local server.
  - Fetches `/api/narrative-objections?refresh=1`.
  - Writes full receipts to `var/narrative-objections-receipts.json`.
- Updated `package.json`:
  - Adds `audit:narrative-objections`.
  - Adds `node --check scripts/narrative-objections.mjs` to `npm run check`.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - `/api/narrative-objections` now serves the latest full receipt-backed objection report by default.
  - `/api/narrative-objections?refresh=1` forces live recomputation.
  - `/api/narrative-objections/:audience` now selects from the cached report by default and supports refresh.
  - Adds `/api/narrative-objections/plan` and `/api/narrative-objections/history`.
  - Terminal `narrative-objections` uses the receipt-backed report when available and names the receipt id.
  - Internal graph/narrative consumers keep defaulting to live objection data.
- Updated `data/runtime-attestation.js`:
  - Adds the new narrative-objection plan/history routes to the public route manifest.
- Updated `scripts/validate-data.mjs` and `test/api-contract.test.mjs`:
  - Validates live and cached objection report shapes.
  - Asserts cache flags, refresh endpoint, plan metadata, history route, cached fixture replay, thin receipt rejection, and live refresh behavior.

## Wave 184 - Narrative Sequence Rich Receipt Hardening

Goal:

- Continue beyond the empty slow frontier by optimizing `/api/narrative-sequence`, the next heatmap hint, by hardening an existing cache so it only replays full rich receipts and keeps `?refresh=1` live recomputation explicit.

Changes:

- Updated `data/narrative-sequence.js`:
  - `buildNarrativeSequenceReportFromReceipt()` now requires a full `report` snapshot instead of accepting split or thin sequence-only receipts.
  - Strictly validates report-level checks, sequence checks, beats, evidence IDs, repair guidance, non-claims, next action, and verification command before replaying.
  - Preserves cached metadata on selected audience sequences: `cachedFromReceipt`, `cachePolicy`, `refreshEndpoint`, and `checkedAt`.
  - Rejects thin or malformed receipts.
- Updated `scripts/narrative-sequence.mjs`:
  - Continues fetching `/api/narrative-sequence?refresh=1`.
  - Writes the full report, plan, contract, repair queue, non-claims, source boundary, side-effect boundary, next action, verification command, and generated timestamp into the local receipt.
  - Preserves check-level `repairAction` and `verificationCommand`.
- Updated `scripts/validate-data.mjs` and `test/api-contract.test.mjs`:
  - Validates rich receipt replay.
  - Asserts thin receipt rejection.
  - Adds live refresh checks for `/api/narrative-sequence?refresh=1`.

- Targeted syntax checks passed for:
  - `data/narrative-sequence.js`
  - `scripts/narrative-sequence.mjs`
  - `scripts/validate-data.mjs`
  - `test/api-contract.test.mjs`
- `node scripts/validate-data.mjs` passed: `Validated 13 projects, 74 claims, 103 artifacts, 13 checks, 41 private references.`
- `npm run sequence:narratives` forced the full refresh route and produced:

## Wave 185 - Route Latency History Payload Summary

Goal:

- Continue beyond the empty slow frontier by optimizing `/api/route-latency/history`, the next heatmap hint, by removing oversized full-receipt payloads from the history route while preserving full receipts on disk.

Changes:

- Updated `data/route-latency.js`:
  - Adds `buildRouteLatencyHistory()`.
  - Returns bounded receipt summaries with top-5 `heatmapPreview` route and terminal samples.
  - Preserves latest summary, receipt ids, slow-frontier snippets, next action, and verification command.
  - Omits full `heatmap` arrays from history responses.
  - Supports a clamped `limit` parameter from 1 to 50.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - `/api/route-latency/history` now returns summarized history via `buildRouteLatencyHistory()`.
  - `/api/route-latency/history?limit=3` returns a smaller bounded summary.
- Updated `scripts/validate-data.mjs` and `test/api-contract.test.mjs`:
  - Validates history mode, summary limit, preview bounds, and absence of full heatmaps.
  - Adds API assertions for default and compact history responses.

  - `var/route-latency-receipts.json` was `6.1M`.

## Wave 190 - Graph Disclosure Links Compact Public Response

Goal:

- Optimize `/api/graph-disclosure-links`, the heatmap target at `78ms` and `110,695` bytes, without starving internal graph consumers of the full relationship set.

Changes:

- Updated `data/graph-disclosure-links.js`:
  - Added `buildGraphDisclosureLinksResponse()`.
  - Public default response now returns `detail: summary` with a relationship preview capped at `30` edges.
  - Preview selection guarantees all five required relation types are represented.
  - Adds `fullDetailEndpoint` and `relationshipPayloadPolicy`.
  - `detail=full` still returns all relationships.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - `/api/graph-disclosure-links` wraps the full report in the compact public response by default.
  - `/api/graph-disclosure-links?detail=full` returns all relationship edges.
  - Internal consumers continue to use the full `currentGraphDisclosureLinksReport()`.
- Updated `scripts/graph-disclosure-links.mjs`:
  - Recorder now fetches `?refresh=1&detail=full` so receipts preserve all graph relationships.
- Updated `scripts/validate-data.mjs` and `test/api-contract.test.mjs`:
  - Validates compact relationship previews, full-detail behavior, and required relation-type coverage.

## Wave 191 - Public Graph Compact Preview

Goal:

- Optimize `/api/graph`, the heatmap target at `65ms` and `217,311` bytes, while preserving complete graph receipts and full-detail access.

Changes:

- Updated `data/graph-snapshot.js`:
  - Added `buildGraphPayloadResponse()`.
  - Default public graph response now returns `detail: summary` with `100` node previews and `120` edge previews.
  - Preview selection preserves `qagent`, all required graph node types, all actual node-type families, all required edge relations, and all relation families before filling the preview.
  - Adds `fullDetailEndpoint` and `graphPayloadPolicy`.
  - `detail=full` still returns every graph node and edge.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - `/api/graph` wraps the full graph in the compact public response by default.
  - `/api/graph?detail=full` returns the complete graph.
  - Internal graph consumers keep using the full graph payload.
- Updated `scripts/graph-snapshot.mjs`:
  - Recorder now fetches `?refresh=1&detail=full` so graph receipts preserve the complete graph.
- Updated `scripts/validate-data.mjs` and `test/api-contract.test.mjs`:
  - Validates compact graph previews, required node/edge coverage, and full-detail behavior.

Evidence:

- Baseline inspection:

## Wave 195 - Route Latency Latest Reader

Goal:

- Optimize `/api/route-latency`, the heatmap target at `41ms` and `13,221` bytes, by avoiding full receipt-store parsing for latest-only reads.

Changes:

- Updated `data/route-latency.js`:
  - Added `readLatestRouteLatencyReceipt()` with a first-receipt JSON object scanner.
  - Exported the latest-only reader while preserving full `readRouteLatencyReceipts()` for history and recorder workflows.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - Wired `currentRouteLatencyReport()` to serve latest and full-detail responses from `readLatestRouteLatencyReceipt()` instead of parsing the full receipt history.
  - Kept `/api/route-latency/history` on the full reader because it intentionally summarizes multiple receipts.

  - `server.js`

  - `/api/route-latency?detail=full` returned `98,795` bytes at `4-5ms`.

  - `node --test test/api-contract.test.mjs` passed: 9 tests, 1 suite, 0 failures, duration `10493.414208ms`.
- Final heatmap receipt:
  - `npm run record:route-latency` produced `route-latency-mpq03ynx 197 route sample(s), 21 terminal sample(s); slow routes 0, slow terminal 0; p95 route 10ms, p95 terminal 3ms; wrote var/route-latency-receipts.json`

## Wave 197 - Route Latency History Cache

Goal:

- Optimize `/api/route-latency/history`, the heatmap target at `12ms` and `45,517` bytes, without losing compact history semantics or `totalAvailable` accuracy.

Changes:

- Updated `data/route-latency.js`:
  - Added `readRouteLatencyHistoryWindow()` for bounded history windows.
  - Added an `mtimeMs:size:limit` history cache so repeated history reads reuse the parsed window until the receipt store changes.
  - Extended `buildRouteLatencyHistory()` with explicit `totalAvailable` support so history responses can return a bounded receipt list while still reporting the full store size.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - Switched `/api/route-latency/history` to `readRouteLatencyHistoryWindow()`.
  - Prewarmed the default route-latency history window before accepting requests.

Evidence:

- Targeted syntax checks passed for:
  - `data/route-latency.js`
  - `server.js`
- History parity verification:
  - Limits `3`, `20`, and `50` returned the same receipt ids as the full reader for each bounded window.
  - `summary.totalAvailable` remained `50`.
  - Repeated `limit=20` history reads hit `0ms` after the mtime cache was warm.
  - An earlier manual scanner version was rejected because first-read timings were slower than the full parse; the final implementation uses the existing full reader once per mtime/limit cache key and keeps the route hot through prewarming.
- Local smoke on a temporary server:
  - `/api/route-latency/history` returned `45,559` bytes and stayed hot at `1-2ms` after client fetch setup.
  - `/api/route-latency/history?limit=3` returned `7,663` bytes and stayed hot at `0-1ms` after its first bounded-window cache fill.
  - `/api/route-latency` returned `13,338` bytes at `0-1ms`.
- Broad validation:
  - `npm run check` passed after revalidating: `Validated 13 projects, 74 claims, 103 artifacts, 13 checks, 41 private references.`
  - `node --test test/api-contract.test.mjs` passed: 9 tests, 1 suite, 0 failures, duration `10369.584291ms`.
- Final heatmap receipt:
  - `npm run record:route-latency` produced `route-latency-mpq0fc4y 197 route sample(s), 21 terminal sample(s); slow routes 0, slow terminal 0; p95 route 6ms, p95 terminal 3ms; wrote var/route-latency-receipts.json`

## Wave 211 - Accessibility Audit History Cache Normalization

Goal:

- Inspect `/api/accessibility-audit/history`, the heatmap target at `30ms` and `1,309` bytes, and determine whether the slowdown was real route work or single-sample timing noise.

Findings:

- The accessibility audit store was only `4.0K` with one report when inspected.
- The route was doing a direct `readAccessibilityAuditReports(root).slice(0, 20)` and returning full reports.
- Claude review confirmed the likely cause: route-latency uses one fresh-loopback sample per route, so a 30ms reading on a tiny 1.3KB endpoint is measurement noise unless repeated across receipts.

Changes:

- Updated `data/accessibility-audit.js`:
  - Added latest-report and bounded-history readers with mtime/size cache keys.
  - Added `buildAccessibilityAuditHistory()` with summarized report history.
  - Kept appended audit reports full-fidelity in `var/accessibility-audit-reports.json`.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - Prewarmed accessibility audit report caches.
  - Switched the command-center `a11y` command to the latest-report scanner.
  - Replaced raw `/api/accessibility-audit/history` reports with compact history.
- Updated `test/api-contract.test.mjs`:
  - Added history mode and summarized-report assertions.

Evidence:

- Targeted syntax checks passed for:
  - `data/accessibility-audit.js`
  - `server.js`
- Direct helper verification:
  - Latest report accepted: `a11y-1779867743468`.
  - Compact accessibility history response: `2,211` bytes with one report and `8` check previews.
  - History reports no longer inline full `checks`.
- Refreshed accessibility audit report:
  - `npm run audit:a11y` produced `a11y-1780007815902 8/8 passed; wrote var/accessibility-audit-reports.json`
- Local smoke on a temporary server:
  - `/api/accessibility-audit/history` returned compact history at `3ms`, `3,478` bytes, and `2` reports.
  - `/api/accessibility-audit/plan` returned at `1ms` and `562` bytes.
- Broad validation:
  - `npm run check` passed after revalidating: `Validated 13 projects, 74 claims, 103 artifacts, 13 checks, 41 private references.`
  - `node --test test/api-contract.test.mjs` passed: 9 tests, 1 suite, 0 failures, duration `3556.388625ms`.
- Final heatmap receipt:
  - `npm run record:route-latency` produced `route-latency-mpq2pzj2 197 route sample(s), 21 terminal sample(s); slow routes 0, slow terminal 0; p95 route 3ms, p95 terminal 2ms; wrote var/route-latency-receipts.json`
  - `/api/accessibility-audit/history` moved to rank `146` at `0ms` and `3,478` bytes.
  - Total measured latency dropped from `577ms` to `228ms`, confirming the prior `30ms` sample was mostly noise.
  - The new heatmap target is `/api/skill-gaps` at `5ms` and `136,481` bytes.
  - The route-latency frontier remains at `0` slow routes and `0` slow terminal commands.

Claude offload:

- Claude supplied the measurement-noise checklist and confirmed the safe optimization shape: summarize history, add bounded/cache readers, keep stored audit reports full, and treat the route-latency sample as a single-point estimate.

## Wave 216 - Graph-Lineage Full-Receipt Cache And Compact History

Goal:

- Optimize `/api/graph-lineage`, the heatmap target at `5ms` and `43,219` bytes, while preserving full graph-lineage internals for graph-depth, graph-confidence-adjacent summaries, self-review, terminal output, and recorder/full-detail consumers.

Changes:

- Updated `data/graph-lineage.js`:
  - Added mtime/size cached latest-receipt and bounded-history readers.
  - Added a valid-full-receipt scanner so compact or legacy receipts cannot poison full cached lineage hydration.
  - Tightened `buildGraphLineageReportFromReceipt()` to reject compact receipts whose actual path arrays do not match summary totals.
  - Added `buildGraphLineageHistory()` with compact receipt summaries and no inlined full `report` payloads.
  - Kept `buildGraphLineageReport()` and `buildGraphLineageResponse(..., { detail: "full" })` preserving full lineage paths.
- Updated `scripts/graph-lineage.mjs`:
  - The recorder now fetches `/api/graph-lineage?refresh=1&detail=full`.
  - Added guards rejecting compact lineage payloads, partial path counts, or compact artifact-gap repair paths.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - Prewarmed graph-lineage receipt caches.
  - Switched cached graph-lineage reads to `readLatestGraphLineageReceipt()`.
  - Passed only the latest valid full receipt into live builds for `latestReceipt` metadata.
  - Replaced raw `/api/graph-lineage/history` receipt output with compact history summaries.
- Updated `scripts/validate-data.mjs` and `test/api-contract.test.mjs`:
  - Added assertions for compact-receipt rejection, full-detail path preservation, compact history mode, cache usability, and no history-inlined reports.

  - The implementation kept those raw-report fields unchanged and applied compaction only at the HTTP response/history edge.

## Wave 222 - Runtime Deploy Readiness Cached Receipt Fast Path

Goal:

- Optimize `/api/runtime-deploy-readiness`, the top route-latency target at `9ms` and `10,405` bytes, while preserving the full default deploy-readiness contract, live refresh semantics, recorder output, runtime chat command behavior, and downstream runtime-evidence-chain consumers.

Changes:

- Updated `data/runtime-deploy-readiness.js`:
  - Added mtime/size cached latest-receipt and bounded-history readers.
  - Added `buildRuntimeDeployReadinessHistory()` with compact receipt summaries that omit full embedded reports.
  - Kept full default `buildRuntimeDeployReadinessReport()` and full recorder receipts intact.
  - Tightened cached receipt validation so default cached responses must keep a real identity hash, private gate status `404`, zero private refresh endpoints, and a passing `surface-contract` check.
  - Made compact history fall back to full report arrays when receipt-level arrays are empty.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - Prewarmed runtime deploy-readiness latest/history receipt caches.
  - Switched default cached reads to `readLatestRuntimeDeployReadinessReceipt()` instead of full receipt-store parsing.
  - Passed only the latest receipt into live builds for latest-receipt metadata.
  - Replaced raw `/api/runtime-deploy-readiness/history` receipts with compact history.
  - Made deploy-readiness and runtime-evidence-chain refresh parsing case-insensitive for `TRUE`/`Live` callers.
  - Let live evidence-chain builds pass their receipt preference through to deploy-readiness, avoiding unnecessary deploy-readiness rebuilds when a cached chain is not available.
- Updated `scripts/validate-data.mjs` and `test/api-contract.test.mjs`:
  - Added compact-history assertions, uppercase refresh coverage, and contract-incompatible receipt rejection coverage.

## Wave 306 - Tier Claim Calibration History

Goal:

- Optimize `/api/evaluation/claim-calibration/history`, the largest remaining public payload at `38,282` bytes, while preserving full claim calibration evidence.

Changes:

- Updated `data/claim-calibration.js`:
  - Removed raw per-receipt `mode`.
  - Replaced repeated per-receipt `checks` arrays with `checkSummary`.
  - Added `repairQueueSummary` and `matrixSummary`.
  - Kept compact `repairQueuePreview` and `matrixPreview` only on the latest receipt; older receipts now carry trend summaries only.
  - Removed repeated calibration detail fields from history matrix preview rows.
  - Added `historyPayloadPolicy` pointing full evidence back to `/api/evaluation/claim-calibration?detail=full`.
  - Fixed history `limit` clamping so `0` floors to `1`.

- Updated `test/api-contract.test.mjs` and `scripts/validate-data.mjs` to verify the compact tiered history shape and limit clamp.

  - Added `buildRuntimeBoundaryHistory`.
  - Added compact history receipts with summary counts, private-gate fields, check IDs, failed check IDs, and repair action IDs.
  - Added top-level definitions for the full report endpoint, private-gate boundary rules, and check verification commands.
  - Added cached history-window reads and robust limit clamping.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - Replaced raw receipt slicing with the compact history builder.
  - Added `?limit=` support for `/api/runtime-boundary/history`.
  - Set the default boundary history limit to 5 receipts.
- Updated `test/api-contract.test.mjs` and `scripts/validate-data.mjs` to assert compact history mode, definitions, ID arrays, omitted raw reports, and the `?limit=0` clamp.

Evidence:

- Direct builder measurement:
  - Raw `/api/runtime-boundary/history` was `35,433` bytes.
  - Compact builder measured `5,627` bytes before refreshing the latest receipt.
  - After refreshing, compact default history measured `6,347` bytes across 4 receipts.
  - `?limit=0` clamps to one row and measured `4,187` bytes.
- Domain recorder:
  - `npm run audit:runtime-boundary` produced `runtime-boundary-mpqj12w0 10/10 check(s) passed; public 199; private 33; score 100/100; wrote var/runtime-boundary-receipts.json`.

  - `/api/runtime-boundary/history?limit=0` returned `200`, `4,187` bytes, `limit: 1`, and 1 compact receipt.
  - `/api/runtime-boundary` still returned `200` with the full cached boundary audit.
- Broad validation:
  - `npm run check` passed after revalidating: `Validated 13 projects, 74 claims, 103 artifacts, 13 checks, 41 private references.`
  - `node --test test/api-contract.test.mjs` passed: 9 tests, 1 suite, 0 failures, duration `3110.669292ms`.

## Wave 346 - True Index Claim Ledger

Goal:

- Optimize `/api/claims`, the largest remaining public payload at `21,937` bytes, while preserving full public claim ledger detail.

Changes:

- Updated `server.js`:
  - Turned the default `/api/claims` response into a true compact index with a 12-row highest-confidence preview.
  - Added all-project `projectClaimSummary` rows so the frontend can keep proof-strength filters accurate without downloading the full claim catalog.
  - Added `statusSummary` and `claimPayloadPolicy` metadata for full catalog and filtered detail access.
  - Preserved full claim rows at `/api/claims?detail=full`, filtered full rows at `?project=` and `?status=`, and individual claim detail at `/api/claims/:id`.
- Updated `command-center.mjs` and regenerated `index.html`:
  - The frontend now reads `projectClaimSummary` for proof filters instead of requiring all 74 claim rows.
  - Fixed the preview wall to synthesize artifact labels and replay targets from compact artifact rows, restoring `case-study replay` text without calling `/api/artifacts?detail=full`.
- Updated `test/api-contract.test.mjs` to assert the compact claim index, project/status summaries, preview limit, and full-catalog preservation.

- Broad validation:

## Wave 350 - Compact Research Rigor History

Goal:

- Optimize `/api/evaluation/research-rigor/history`, the largest remaining public payload at `21,629` bytes, while preserving full research-rigor receipts.

Changes:

- Updated `data/research-rigor.js`:
  - Changed the history default from a 20-receipt response to a five-receipt compact summary.
  - Added `detail`, `compact`, `fullDetailEndpoint`, and `historyPayloadPolicy` to match the other compact history contracts.
  - Preserved raw local receipts at `/api/evaluation/research-rigor/history?detail=full`.
  - Added summary definitions that expose the plan, full report endpoint, refresh endpoint, full history endpoint, compact fields, omitted fields, dimension ids, gradebook ids, and check ids.
  - Removed verbose check detail prose from compact `checkPreview` rows while keeping the full check detail in full history.
- Updated `server.js` so `/api/evaluation/research-rigor/history` defaults to `limit=5`, while `?detail=full` defaults to the existing full-history limit.
- Updated `test/api-contract.test.mjs` and `scripts/validate-data.mjs` to assert compact summary history, full-history preservation, and old-receipt compatibility for historical receipts that predate `evaluationGradebook`.

Evidence:

- Initial upstream gate failure found and repaired:
  - `npm run audit:research-rigor` first wrote `research-rigor-mpqnvky1` but failed at `7/11` checks, `3/6` dimensions, `4/8` grades, `59/100`.
  - Root cause was stale/weak upstream runtime and design evidence, not the history split: runtime chain `68/100`, design ambition `83/100`, stress `6/8`, integrity `7/8`.
  - Refreshed the upstream evidence chain:
    - `npm run record:runtime` produced `runtime-mpqnwups readiness 93/100; 5 runtime change(s)`.
    - `npm run record:runtime-surface` produced `surface-mpqnwx06 240/240 passed; 33/33 private gate probe(s)`.
    - `npm run refresh:evidence` produced `refresh-1780043459072 51/51 passed`.
    - `npm run explain:runtime` produced `runtime-explain-mpqnx1r0 9/9 check(s) passed; 6 proof claim(s); 6 blocked claim(s); 6 audit step(s); score 100/100`.
    - `npm run audit:runtime-deploy` produced `runtime-deploy-readiness-mpqnx40y 8/8 check(s) passed; 6/6 gate(s); score 100/100`.
    - `npm run audit:runtime-chain` then produced `runtime-chain-mpqnx899 14/14 check(s) passed; 8 link(s); 6/6 parity claim(s) blocked; score 100/100`.
    - `npm run audit:design-ambition` then produced `design-ambition-mpqnxapc 11/11 check(s) passed; 6/6 family/families; 7/7 stable surface(s); score 100/100`.
    - `npm run stress:evaluation` then produced `research-stress-mpqnxe3v 8/8 scenario(s) passed; score 99/100`.
    - `npm run audit:evaluation-integrity` then produced `evaluation-integrity-mpqnxgsf 8/8 check(s) passed; 6/6 domain(s); score 100/100`.
  - Final `npm run audit:research-rigor` produced `research-rigor-mpqnxk2v 11/11 check(s) passed; 6/6 dimension(s); 8/8 grade(s); min C+; score 100/100`.
- Local API smoke on a temporary server:
  - `/api/evaluation/research-rigor/history` returned `200`, `14,978` bytes, `detail: summary`, `compact: true`, 5 receipts, latest `research-rigor-mpqnxk2v`.

## Wave 378 - Compact Artifact Museum Comparison Matrix

Goal:

Changes:

## Wave 446 - Compact Artifact Gap Repair Defaults

Goal:

- Optimize `/api/artifact-gap-repair`, the largest remaining public payload at `10,249` bytes, while preserving full artifact gap repair evidence.

Changes:

- Updated `data/artifact-gap-proof-repair.js`:
  - Added `buildArtifactGapProofRepairResponse(report, { detail })`.
  - Made the public default a compact summary with repair item counts, audience blockers, unlock counts, guard counts, repair path counts, and command availability flags.
  - Preserved full repair paths, forbidden claim text, check detail, repair actions, and verification commands at `/api/artifact-gap-repair?detail=full`.
  - Added `artifactGapRepairPayloadPolicy`, `fullDetailEndpoint`, and compact plan metadata.
- Updated `server.js`:
  - Wrapped `/api/artifact-gap-repair` with the compact/full response builder.
- Updated `scripts/artifact-gap-repair.mjs`:
  - Changed the recorder fetch to `/api/artifact-gap-repair?refresh=1&detail=full` and asserted that receipts are built from the full payload.
- Updated `test/api-contract.test.mjs` and `scripts/validate-data.mjs`:
  - Asserted compact defaults omit forbidden claim text, repair prose, check detail, and verification commands while exposing availability/count fields.
  - Asserted full detail keeps forbidden claim text and verification commands.

Goal:
